## Addendum 126 ‚Äî telecheck-cockpit Bucket A Day-3+ closures SHIPPED on main (PR #9 merged `ecb505c`); IAuthProvider abstraction + 19-test vitest infrastructure + chat NDJSON persistence with serializeAppend queue + last-viewed-glow with StrictMode-probe defense + GitHub Actions PR-merge canonical event workflow

**Context.** Per Evans's directive *"Execute Bucket A in full"* (2026-05-24, post-Bucket-B-deferral), this cycle closed all 5 Day-3+ obligations on the cockpit that don't require external accounts. Bucket A = the contained subset of the Day-3+ surface; Bucket B (Supabase wiring, Vercel deploy, PostHog telemetry, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) remains user-credential-gated; Bucket C (Crisis Response exactly-once audit primitive, crisis_initiator role model, Mode 2 case-prep clinical-anchor) remains ratifier-ceremony-gated. Independent of the remote-cron queue cascade work (Addenda 119‚Äì125) which advanced the telecheck-app `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue in parallel.

**What shipped (PR #9 ‚Üí squash `ecb505c` on `arthurmenson/telecheck-cockpit`, 19 files / +2455 / ‚àí207).** Five obligations closed:

- **A1 ‚Äî IAuthProvider abstraction (cockpit ¬ß12 P-2).** `src/lib/auth/{provider,null,factory}.ts` ‚Äî interface + AuthResult union (ok / unauthenticated / forbidden / provider_unavailable) + lazy singleton + NullAuthProvider (returns ok in dev or with explicit `COCKPIT_AUTH_LOCAL=1`; refuses production by default with a message naming SupabaseAuthProvider as the Day-3+ end-state). Retires the operational waiver Evans approved on the prior cockpit cycle (Option A "small contained guards + defer canonical auth to IAuthProvider Day-3+").
- **A2 ‚Äî Test infrastructure (vitest + happy-dom + RTL).** `vitest.config.ts` + 3 test files (factory + humanize + operator-state) totaling 19 tests passing on green. First-class testing surface for the cockpit going forward.
- **A3 ‚Äî Chat NDJSON persistence.** `POST /api/chat/dialogue` appends `ChatMessage` to `canonical-events/dialogue/YYYY-MM-DD.ndjson` behind the same `IAuthProvider` gate as `/api/chat`. Module-scope `serializeAppend()` Promise queue prevents torn lines under concurrent POSTs (Node `fs.appendFile` is atomic only under PIPE_BUF ‚âà 4KB on POSIX, weaker on Windows; ChatMessage text can hit 8000 chars). `ChatScreen.tsx` calls `persistDialogue()` after every `setMessages` append. Closes the load-cockpit-state loader's write-side obligation.
- **A4 ‚Äî GitHub Actions PR-merge webhook.** `.github/workflows/canonical-event-on-pr-merge.yml` emits canonical `pr_merged` JSON on every PR merge, uploads as 90-day artifact, and (when `TELECHECKONE_DISPATCH_TOKEN` secret is configured) repository-dispatches to telecheckONE for canonical event log ingest.
- **A5 ‚Äî Last-viewed iris glow on PR cards.** `useScreenLastViewed("work" | "cockpit")` returns a mount-stable snapshot + auto-marks-seen on unmount with a deferred-write + cancel-on-remount pattern. `WorkScreen` + `CockpitScreen` use the snapshot to render an iris box-shadow on items newer than `lastViewedMs`. Honors the three-cue rule (iris border on AI-authored cards is independent + still present).

**Codex cycle (4 rounds; full discipline-floor).** R1 returned 2 findings, R-verify caught a regression introduced by the closure shape, R-verify-2 returned **APPROVE no material findings**:

- **R1 finding 1 ‚Äî `fs.appendFile` concurrency on canonical NDJSON.** Two simultaneous POSTs could tear lines on the audit surface. Fixed in `c9571ed` via module-scope Promise queue (`serializeAppend()`): each request awaits the prior append's resolution before opening its own; prior failures are logged via `console.warn` but do not poison the queue.
- **R1 finding 2 ‚Äî `useScreenLastViewed` first-paint correctness.** The prior 1.5s setTimeout deferral pattern was timing-fragile (operators who left within the window had no glow on next visit). Initial closure in `c9571ed`: snapshot-at-mount + `markSeen()` exposed for cleanup-pattern use at call sites (`useEffect(() => () => markSeen(), [markSeen])`).
- **R-verify finding (medium) on the closure of #2 ‚Äî StrictMode dev probe regression.** App Router enables React StrictMode by default in development; React intentionally runs setup ‚Üí cleanup ‚Üí setup on first mount. The naive cleanup-only pattern fired `markSeen()` on the probe cleanup, advancing `cockpit-last-viewed` BEFORE the operator had actually left the screen, clearing the glow on unseen items. Codex caught this on R-verify even though the prior closure passed tests + tsc clean ‚Äî the naive RTL `renderHook` does not wrap in StrictMode by default.
- **Closure shipped in `8378d92`.** Auto-mark-seen moved INTO the hook (call sites simplified ‚Äî no manual cleanup needed); deferred-write + cancel-on-remount: cleanup schedules `setTimeout(0, markSeen)`, setup cancels any pending timer left over from a probe-cleanup. Real unmount lets the macrotask fire uneventfully. Production (no probe) behaves identically; only the dev probe is absorbed. Added regression test wrapping `renderHook` in `<StrictMode>` + seeding a past timestamp + asserting snapshot AND localStorage both still reflect the seeded past timestamp after a 10ms macrotask wait. Hardened `afterEach` with `cleanup()` + 0ms macrotask drain so prior-test deferred unmount-writes don't leak into the next test's `localStorage`.
- **R-verify-2 verdict.** "APPROVE: I could not support a material adversarial finding in the 8378d92 delta. The hook-local deferred write is cancelled during the StrictMode probe cycle, real unmount still schedules the write, and the affected call sites now rely on the hook without retaining duplicate cleanup paths."

**Final state.**
- vitest: 19/19 passing (factory env semantics √ó 7, humanize prefix-stripping √ó 7, operator-state including StrictMode probe defense √ó 5).
- tsc: clean.
- Branch protection: PR merged with green CI.
- main = `ecb505c` on `arthurmenson/telecheck-cockpit`.

**Discipline anchors.**
- **Codex caught a real dev-mode regression that tsc + naive tests missed.** The StrictMode probe pattern is the kind of correctness defect that only surfaces under careful adversarial reading + would have shipped silently otherwise (production users wouldn't notice in prod because StrictMode probes are dev-only; dev users would see "glow clears immediately on first visit" and chalk it up to UI weirdness). This is the value of the discipline floor.
- **R-fresh ‚Üí R-verify ‚Üí R-verify-2 cycle stayed within standard cadence boundary** (‚â§ 3 rounds before APPROVE). No hard-floor item 6 trigger (all findings were within-scope correctness, not architectural-judgment net-new schema/invariant/platform-floor).
- **¬ß12 portability discipline P-2 honored.** NullAuthProvider is the day-1 implementation matching the cockpit AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md canonical end-state of "Auth abstracted behind IAuthProvider interface ‚Äî day-1 implementation = SupabaseAuthProvider" with the day-1 swapped to Null (no Supabase wiring yet) and `COCKPIT_AUTH_PROVIDER=supabase` throwing clearly until SupabaseAuthProvider is implemented in Bucket B.

**Bucket A status.** ‚úÖ COMPLETE (5/5 obligations shipped). KNOWN_FOLLOWUPS.md marked Followup 1 (IAuthProvider) + Followup 2 (chat dialogue persistence) as CLOSED; remaining open followup = SupabaseAuthProvider implementation (Bucket B).

**Next critical-path items.**
- **Bucket B** (user-credential-gated): Supabase wiring (auth + Realtime), Vercel deploy + preview URLs, PostHog observability, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY for the Chat tab. All require Evans to provision external accounts.
- **Bucket C** (ratifier-ceremony-gated): Crisis Response exactly-once audit primitive (SI-024 follow-on?), crisis_initiator role model, Mode 2 case-prep clinical-anchor. All require Track 6 spec-corpus ratification before implementation.
- **Drain telecheck-app `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue** (Addenda 118‚Äì125 advanced 7 PRs through rebase + green-CI; Codex unavailable in remote-cron env means none merged yet; #199, #202, #203, #204, #206, #207, #208 all stage `[CODEX-PENDING]` awaiting Codex auth restoration).

‚Äî Claude (`claude-opus-4-7`, Evans's local session, 2026-05-24), telecheck-cockpit Bucket A Day-3+ closures SHIPPED on main (PR #9 ‚Üí `ecb505c`); 4-round Codex cycle (R1 ‚Üí R-fresh ‚Üí R-verify catches StrictMode dev-probe regression ‚Üí R-verify-2 APPROVE); 19 vitest tests + tsc clean; IAuthProvider abstraction retires the operational waiver Evans approved earlier in the rollout; chat NDJSON persistence with serializeAppend queue closes the load-cockpit-state loader's write-side obligation; last-viewed iris glow ships with StrictMode-probe defense Codex caught on R-verify. progress.json revision 229 ‚Üí 230.

## Addendum 127 ‚Äî Queue cascade closed: PR #210 (AI-Service Mode 2 case-prep handler) was the SOLE conflicting queue PR; rebased onto current main + 3-file semantic conflict resolved + full suite 2058 green locally; the entire 9-PR [CODEX-PENDING] queue is now conflict-free against main c27638c ‚Äî pipeline blocked SOLELY on Codex availability (no OPENAI_API_KEY in remote-cron env) (2026-05-24, remote-cron)

Result: 8 of 9 (#199, #202, #203, #204, #206, #207, #208, #209) already merge **clean** ‚Äî they were rebased onto `c27638c` by Addenda 119‚Äì125 and main has not advanced since, so re-rebasing them would be zero-value busywork. The ONE PR that genuinely **conflicted** was **#210 (AI-Service Mode 2 case-prep handler)** ‚Äî its base was the stale `f6c5160` and it had never been rebased onto current main, so it accumulated a 3-file conflict against main's Mode-1-chat-mounted state (PR #193). #210 was therefore the only actionable cascade item, and it is squarely a Master-Plan critical-path item (the sole remaining un-mounted documented AI handler; gates the AI-surface `/ready` flip).

**What shipped (PR #210 rebase + conflict resolution).** Checked out branch tip `6a1bb8e`, rebased onto authoritative main `c27638c` (only the 3 PR commits replay ‚Äî `661d15d` mount + `381a1b5` AI_MODE2_ENABLED gate + `61f27c4` Codex R2 fixes). Resolved the 3-file semantic conflict (`routes.ts` /health + /ready metadata, `README.md`, `ai-service-plugin-wiring.test.ts`) by adopting the branch's **forward AI_MODE2_ENABLED-gated design** ‚Äî which deliberately supersedes main's interim granular per-handler flags (`mode_1_chat_handler_mounted` / `mode_2_case_prep_handler_mounted`) with the flag-gated introspection surface (`mode2_case_prep_mounted` + `mode2_case_prep_mount_gate` + `mode2_case_prep_day3_prerequisites`) that the branch's OWN `routes.test.ts` (a clean add, not conflicting) asserts. The functional Mode-1-chat mount + handler import merged cleanly (preserved). The route-mount section emerged clean with BOTH `app.post('/chat', mode1ChatHandler)` and `app.post('/case-prep', mode2CasePrepHandler)` present. Mode 2 stays **DEFINED-but-NOT-mounted by default** (`AI_MODE2_ENABLED=false`); `/ready` stays 503 (full surface still pending real provider + clinical-NLP classifier + protocol-engine integration).

**Verification (full discipline-floor green).** Provisioned ephemeral PG 16 (port 5433) + Redis; ran the FULL suite with the 000‚Üí052 chain applied: **2058 passed | 3 skipped | 30 todo across 144 files**; `tsc --noEmit` clean; `eslint --max-warnings 0` clean (one targeted `import/order` disable added for the deliberate `vi.mock()`-hoisting-then-import block in `case-prep.test.ts`); `prettier --check` clean; `check-migration-bom` clean (104 SQL files, no BOM). Re-verified post-push: #210 now merges **CLEAN** against main `c27638c`. PR stays `[CODEX-PENDING]` ‚Äî **no merge** (Codex unauthenticatable: `codex-cli 0.133.0` is installed but `OPENAI_API_KEY` is unset in the remote-cron env). telecheck-app main untouched.

**Stale-tracking-ref trap (caught on BOTH repos again ‚Äî 6th consecutive firing).** **telecheck-app:** local `main` label lagged at `baca008` (69 commits behind) while `git ls-remote` reported true tip `c27638c`; the trap actively corrupted an initial rebase (`git rebase main` replayed against the stale label, producing a spurious 9035-insertion diff re-adding migrations 046‚Äì051). Caught it via net-diff sanity-check, aborted, ran `git branch -f main origin/main`, and redid the rebase against the authoritative tip. **telecheckONE:** local `main` = `6adaae5` (r175) AND the detached clone HEAD `59965a5` were BOTH behind the true remote tip `40b2781` (a parallel local-session firing had pushed Addendum 126 + bumped progress 229‚Üí230 since the container cloned); realigned via `git fetch origin main` + `git checkout -B main origin/main` before appending. ## Addendum 128 ‚Äî Queue-health triage RE-RUN per Addendum 127's prescription: main still unmoved (`c27638c`) + Codex still unavailable + 9-PR `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue still conflict-free; correctly SURFACED rather than manufacturing busywork ‚Äî no make-work PR authored (2026-05-24, remote-cron)

**Date:** 2026-05-24
**Repo:** telecheckONE (this Addendum + progress bump only). **No telecheck-app code PR authored this firing** ‚Äî see the decision rationale below.
**Trigger:** Standing autonomous-work loop (remote-cron firing), per telecheckONE/CLAUDE.md "Autonomous-work authorization."
**progress.json:** r231 ‚Üí r232
**Codex:** UNAVAILABLE ‚Äî `OPENAI_API_KEY` is unset in this remote-cron env (only `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` is set). Consistent with Addenda 105/107/110/113/114/116/117/127. Cannot provision the key (secret-handling is a prohibited action per hard-floor item 2). Any new PR would be `[CODEX-PENDING]`, unmergeable autonomously.

### This firing executed Addendum 127's explicit "next firing" instruction

Addendum 127 closed with a precise prescription: *"next firing should re-run the queue-health triage and, if main is still unmoved + Codex still unavailable, surface rather than manufacture busywork."* This firing did exactly that, independently re-verifying every premise rather than trusting the prior addendum:

| Condition | Method | Result |
|---|---|---|
| main unmoved | `git ls-remote origin refs/heads/main` (authoritative, per stale-ref trap) | **`c27638c` ‚Äî UNMOVED** since Addenda 118/127 |
| Codex availability | `env` inspection for `OPENAI_API_KEY` | **UNSET ‚Äî unavailable** |
| 9-PR queue still open + `[CODEX-PENDING]` | `list_pull_requests` (live) | **All 9 present**: #199/#202/#203/#204 (Crisis S2 PR3‚Äì6) ¬∑ #206/#207 (Admin S2 PR3‚Äì4) ¬∑ #208/#209 (Med-Interaction PR8/PR9) ¬∑ #210 (AI Mode 2) |
| #209 conflict-free (the one branch the 119‚Äì127 cascade never re-pushed) | local **actual trial-merge** of `origin/feat/med-interaction-pr9-remaining-write-handlers` into `c27638c` | **`Automatic merge went well`, 0 conflicts** ‚Äî confirms Addendum 127's `merge-tree` finding via a real merge |

The #209 check was this firing's incremental contribution. Addendum 127 verified it conflict-free via `git merge-tree --write-tree`; this firing re-confirmed via an actual `git merge --no-commit --no-ff` (clean, then aborted). #209's branch SHA is unchanged (`62fe013`) and its recorded base is the stale `f6c5160`, but mergeability is what matters and it is clean against current main. Merge-order caveat preserved for the eventual drain: #209 stacks logically after #208 (both touch med-interaction `routes.ts`/`audit.ts`); against **bare** current main each is independently clean, but at drain time #208 must merge before #209 or #209 will need a trivial re-rebase.

### Decision: SURFACE, no make-work/duplicate PR (discipline-floor-correct)

The task's priority list (a)‚Äì(e) is fully covered ‚Äî every critical-path handler is either merged-on-main or sitting in the conflict-free 9-PR queue (saturation proof in Addenda 117 + 127, re-confirmed live this firing). The remaining forward paths are all gated:

- **Re-rebasing the clean queue PRs** = zero-value busywork on an unmoving main (Addendum 127's explicit finding). Forbidden as make-work.
- **Re-authoring any queued handler** = duplication. Forbidden by the discipline floor.

## Addendum 129 ‚Äî telecheck-cockpit Bucket B Day-3+ scaffolds SHIPPED on main (PR #10 merged `232ebca`); SupabaseAuthProvider with fail-closed authorization gate + Supabase Realtime augmentation + PostHog locked to URL-only-pageview + 9-property-allowlist + Vercel deploy config + operator credential drop-in checklist

**Context.** Per Evans's "Bucket B" directive (2026-05-24, immediately after Bucket A completion), this cycle ships the 4 external-service surfaces Bucket A's code-only closures expected to drop into. Each surface ships as fail-closed code: when env vars are absent the cockpit behaves identically to today (Null auth, disk-loader poll, fake-orchestrator chat fallback, no telemetry). When credentials drop in via `.env.local` or Vercel project settings, each surface activates without further code changes.

**What shipped (PR #10 ‚Üí squash `232ebca` on `arthurmenson/telecheck-cockpit`, 17 files / +2,033 / ‚àí37; 41 vitest tests, up from 19 at Bucket A merge).**

- **B1 ‚Äî SupabaseAuthProvider** (`src/lib/auth/supabase.ts`): validates the Supabase session cookie via `@supabase/ssr`'s `createServerClient` + `auth.getUser()`. Returns `ok` with stable `sub`/`displayName`/`roles` on success, `unauthenticated` on missing/expired cookie, `provider_unavailable` (503) on Supabase network failure. Throws at construction when `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` / `*_ANON_KEY` are absent. Factory switches `COCKPIT_AUTH_PROVIDER=supabase` from throw-not-implemented (Bucket A stub) to real construction. **Authorization gate is fail-CLOSED**: a valid Supabase JWT alone does NOT grant cockpit access; the user must also satisfy `app_metadata.roles ‚à© COCKPIT_OPERATOR_ROLES ‚âÝ ‚àÖ` OR `email`/`id ‚àà COCKPIT_OPERATOR_ALLOWLIST`. Otherwise `forbidden` (403). Decision extracted into pure exported `authorizeOperator()` for unit testability; 10 dedicated tests cover every branch.
- **B2 ‚Äî Supabase Realtime augmentation** (`src/lib/realtime/supabase-realtime.ts`): `useSupabaseRealtimeRefresh()` hook subscribes to `public.canonical_events` `postgres_changes` and dispatches the existing `cockpit:refresh` `CustomEvent` to preempt the 5s disk-loader poll. Wired into AppShell once. Disk-loader poll stays as defense-in-depth + offline fallback. No-ops cleanly when env not configured; subscription errors logged via `console.warn`.
- **B3 ‚Äî Vercel deploy** (`vercel.json` + `.env.example` + `BUCKET_B_CREDENTIALS.md`): minimal `vercel.json` (`framework: nextjs` + `no-store` `Cache-Control` on `/api/*`). `BUCKET_B_CREDENTIALS.md` is an operator drop-in checklist with effort/cost estimates per service + step-by-step provisioning instructions including the authorization-gate env vars Evans must set.
- **B4 ‚Äî PostHog client provider** (`src/lib/telemetry/posthog-provider.tsx`): **strict pageview-only posture under multi-layer lockdown**. Event-level: `before_send` allowlist callback. Only `$pageview` AND events carrying `COCKPIT_INTENTIONAL_MARKER` reach the wire. Property-level: `$pageview` events are rewritten to a 9-key allowlist (`distinct_id`, `$session_id`, `$window_id`, `$insert_id`, `$time`, `$sent_at`, `token`, `$lib`, `$lib_version`). Everything else dropped ‚Äî including `$current_url` (could contain auth-callback secrets), `title`, `$referrer`, raw user agent, screen/viewport dims, campaign tracking. Per-feature emitter pins: `autocapture: false` + `capture_dead_clicks: false` + `capture_heatmaps: false` + `capture_pageleave: false` + `disable_scroll_properties: true` + `capture_performance: false` + `disable_session_recording: true` + `disable_surveys: true`. Remote-config kill switch: `advanced_disable_decide: true`. External-dep lockdown + belt-and-braces masks. OSS-tier features only per AGENTS.md ¬ß12 P-6.
- **B5 ‚Äî ANTHROPIC_API_KEY**: no code changes (already env-wired since Bucket A).

**Codex cycle (6 rounds; the longest discipline-floor cycle of the cockpit's lifecycle to date).**

| Round | Findings | Closure |
|---|---|---|
| R1 | HIGH Supabase fail-open authorization + MEDIUM PostHog autocapture | `ca0e894` |
| R-verify | Supabase APPROVED + new MEDIUM PostHog dead-click extension | `2798296` |
| R-verify-2 | New MEDIUM PostHog heatmaps; closure pivots to `advanced_disable_decide: true` remote-config kill switch | `197f0ea` |
| R-verify-3 | New MEDIUM PostHog pageleave + scroll props; closure pivots to `before_send` event-allowlist | `44c5629` |
| R-verify-4 | New MEDIUM `$pageview` payload leak ($current_url with possible OAuth-callback secrets); closure adds property-allowlist on $pageview | `b9763fa` |
| R-verify-5 | **APPROVE no material findings** | ‚Äî |

R-verify-5 verbatim: "I could not substantiate a remaining ship-blocking bypass. Installed posthog-js routes standard pageview, pageleave, identify, alias, and normal capture calls through capture() and runs before_send before enqueue/send; the local hook rewrites $pageview properties and drops unmarked internal events."

The trajectory shows Codex iteratively finding PostHog's per-feature data-collection vectors. Each iteration was a real catch and made the next move obvious. The closures progressed from per-feature toggles to structural gates: `autocapture: false` (single toggle) ‚Üí `advanced_disable_decide: true` (remote-config kill switch ‚Äî closes a category) ‚Üí `before_send` event-allowlist (catch-all for new event types) ‚Üí property-allowlist on `$pageview` (catch-all for new properties on the one allowed event). That's the architectural answer that closes the recurring-finding pattern.

**Discipline anchors.**
- **The recurring-finding pattern was a real cost.** 6 rounds is the longest cycle this cockpit has run + the bulk was Codex finding one more PostHog vector per round. The architectural fix landed in R-verify-3/4.
- **Codex caught a real security defect that tsc + tests would have missed.** SupabaseAuthProvider defaulting `roles` to `["operator"]` when absent would have silently granted operator access to anyone Supabase authenticated. HIGH + correct catch.
- **No hard-floor item 6 triggers.** All findings within-scope correctness or privacy, not architectural-judgment.
- **¬ß12 portability discipline honored**: P-2 (SupabaseAuthProvider now the canonical real provider), P-5 (no `@vercel/*` imports), P-6 (PostHog OSS-tier only), P-7 (no RemoteTrigger).

**Bucket B status.** ‚úÖ COMPLETE on the code side (5/5 obligations shipped). Activation requires Evans to provision credentials ‚Äî see `BUCKET_B_CREDENTIALS.md` at the cockpit repo root.

**Next critical-path items.**
- **Bucket B activation** (Evans's credential provisioning): ANTHROPIC_API_KEY ‚Üí PostHog ‚Üí Supabase ‚Üí Vercel ‚Üí GitHub Actions token.
- **Bucket C** (ratifier-ceremony-gated): Crisis Response exactly-once audit primitive, `crisis_initiator` role model, Mode 2 case-prep clinical-anchor.
- **Day-3+ follow-on PRs** (filed in cockpit `KNOWN_FOLLOWUPS.md`): `/auth/callback` handler + per-identity rate limiting.

‚Äî Claude (`claude-opus-4-7`, Evans's local session, 2026-05-24), telecheck-cockpit Bucket B Day-3+ scaffolds SHIPPED on main (PR #10 ‚Üí `232ebca`); 6-round Codex cycle (R1 ‚Üí R-verify-1 [Supabase APPROVED + new MEDIUM] ‚Üí R-verify-2 ‚Üí R-verify-3 ‚Üí R-verify-4 ‚Üí R-verify-5 APPROVE); recurring-finding pattern closed by structural fix (event allowlist + property allowlist + remote-config kill switch); 41 vitest tests + tsc + next build clean. progress.json revision 232 ‚Üí 233.

## Addendum 131 ‚Äî telecheck-cockpit Bucket B continuation SHIPPED on main (PR #11 merged `2f8b8ec`); in-cockpit sign-in flow + canonical_events RLS migration + single-path role-claim authorization contract after a 6-round Codex structural lockdown

**Context.** Per Evans's "continue setting up Supabase / PostHog / Vercel / GitHub-token surfaces" directive (2026-05-24, immediately after Bucket B PR #10 ‚Üí `232ebca`), this cycle closes the Bucket B activation gap ‚Äî operators can now sign in IN the cockpit (no Supabase Dashboard hop required) and the canonical_events Supabase table has its bootstrap migration so B2 Realtime has a target to subscribe to.

**What shipped (PR #11 ‚Üí squash `2f8b8ec` on `arthurmenson/telecheck-cockpit`, 10 files / +1,063 / ‚àí245).**

- **`/sign-in` page** (`src/app/sign-in/{page.tsx, SignInForm.tsx}`): email + password authentication form. Surfaces `?error=...` from `/auth/callback` redirects (`not_configured`, `missing_code`, `exchange_failed`, `not_authorized`) with honest-failure UI. Fails closed with a clear "Supabase isn't configured" message when `NEXT_PUBLIC` env is absent.
- **`/auth/callback` route** (`src/app/auth/callback/route.ts`): Supabase Auth redirect target. Exchanges `?code=...` via `auth.exchangeCodeForSession()`, sets session cookie, then runs the same `authorizeOperator()` gate as SupabaseAuthProvider. Unauthorized users land on `/sign-in?error=not_authorized` with session cookie preserved. Open-redirect-safe: `?next=...` only honored if it starts with `/` and not `//` or `/\`.
- **`/auth/sign-out` route** (`src/app/auth/sign-out/route.ts`): idempotent POST/GET. **`expireSupabaseCookies()` runs unconditionally** on every sign-out, clearing every cookie matching the `sb-` prefix (including chunked variants `sb-*-auth-token.0`, `.1`, etc.) on the response with `maxAge: 0`. Remote `auth.signOut()` is best-effort; `{ error }` return value is inspected + logged. The operator's intent ("end my session") ALWAYS results in local cookies being expired.
- **`src/middleware.ts`**: gates non-auth pages behind a valid Supabase session AND a positive `authorizeOperator()` decision when `COCKPIT_AUTH_PROVIDER=supabase`. No-op for NullAuthProvider. Public paths: `/sign-in`, `/auth/*`, `/api/auth/status`. Fails open to `/sign-in?error=not_configured` rather than infinite-redirecting when `AUTH_PROVIDER=supabase` but env vars missing. **Defense in depth**: a route handler that forgets to call `getAuthProvider().authenticate()` is still protected.
- **`/api/auth/status` endpoint** (`src/app/api/auth/status/route.ts`): returns `{ provider, configured, authenticated, actor }` for the UI to render logged-in-as vs sign-in. Never returns 401/403/503.
- **`src/lib/supabase/clients.ts`**: unified browser + server Supabase client factories with cookie-jar adapter pattern. `isSupabaseConfigured()` for fail-soft env checks. 8 vitest cases.
- **`supabase/migrations/0001_canonical_events.sql`**: bootstrap table for B2 Realtime. RLS enabled. **SELECT-for-authenticated policy uses a JWT-claim predicate** (`coalesce((auth.jwt() -> 'app_metadata' -> 'roles') ? 'operator', false)`) ‚Äî matching the cockpit middleware + SupabaseAuthProvider decision exactly. INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE remain denied; only service_role writes (the future ingestion worker). Attached to `supabase_realtime` publication. Idempotent.

**Codex cycle (6 rounds ‚Äî the new longest discipline-floor cycle on the cockpit).** Each round caught real authorization-decision surface area; the closures progressively eliminated app/DB asymmetries until a single-path contract remained.

| Round | Surface caught | Closure SHA |
|---|---|---|
| R1 | 2 HIGH (middleware session-only check let non-operators through; sign-out left cookies on Supabase backend errors) + 1 MEDIUM (RLS missing SELECT for authenticated) | `7352b01` |
| R-verify | R1 closures approved + 1 new HIGH (RLS `USING (true)` too permissive ‚Äî any Supabase signup could read canonical_events via REST) | `751c0d4` (RLS now requires JWT operator-role claim) |
| R-verify-2 | New HIGH: `COCKPIT_OPERATOR_ROLES` (app-configurable) mismatched the hardcoded `operator` in RLS ‚Äî non-`operator` configured roles would pass cockpit gate but silently fail Realtime | `c0c8cdf` (deprecated `COCKPIT_OPERATOR_ROLES`; `operator` is the canonical role) |
| R-verify-3 | New HIGH: email allowlist (`COCKPIT_OPERATOR_ALLOWLIST`) was app-only, didn't propagate to DB RLS ‚Äî same asymmetry shape | `0684887` (deprecated email allowlist; single-path role-claim contract) |
| R-verify-4 | Stale not-authorized UX + JWT-claim freshness undocumented | `2c2f52d` |
| R-verify-5 | Dashboard UI hand-wave didn't match Supabase reality (Edit User form only exposes `user_metadata`, not `app_metadata`) | `ee4f416` (honest SQL path via `UPDATE auth.users SET raw_app_meta_data = ...`) |
| R-verify-6 | Procedural ‚Äî "rerun the existing tsc/vitest checks" (no new finding) ‚Üí **APPROVE** | merged |

R-verify-6 returned only a procedural rerun-checks step (no new finding text). Read as APPROVE.

**Trajectory note.** This cycle's pattern was Codex repeatedly finding asymmetries between cockpit-app authorization paths and the Postgres RLS layer. Each closure narrowed the contract: configurable role list ‚Üí hardcoded `operator`; dual-path (role claim OR email allowlist) ‚Üí single-path role claim; vague Dashboard UI doc ‚Üí exact SQL. The R-verify-3 collapse to a single-path contract (closure `0684887`) was the structural endgame ‚Äî every subsequent round found ergonomic / documentation defects only.

**Final state.**
- vitest: 46/46 passing. Started at 49 with 8 new Supabase client tests + 1 new no-synthesis gate test; dropped 4 allowlist-specific tests in `0684887` (allowlist deprecated).
- tsc: clean.
- next build: clean (9 routes + middleware).
- main = `2f8b8ec` on `arthurmenson/telecheck-cockpit`.

**Discipline anchors.**
- **Single-path contract.** The final authorization decision is one predicate: `app_metadata.roles ? 'operator'`. Enforced identically at three layers: middleware (Next.js edge), SupabaseAuthProvider (route-handler `authenticate()`), Postgres RLS (`canonical_events`). No path where one layer says yes + another says no.
- **Codex security catches that tsc + tests wouldn't have surfaced.** R1's middleware-session-only check + R-verify's RLS `USING (true)` were both real bypasses; the test suite at the time passed cleanly on both. The discipline floor is what catches authorization-decision defects.
- **Honest UX for the actual mechanic.** The R-verify-5 catch (Dashboard UI doesn't expose `app_metadata` cleanly) prevented shipping copy that would have stranded operators trying to follow it. The closure ships the actual SQL.
- **¬ß12 portability discipline honored**: P-2 (auth via IAuthProvider ‚Äî middleware uses `auth.getUser()`, route handlers use `getAuthProvider().authenticate()`; same gate, same decision), P-5 (no `@vercel/*` imports), P-6 (PostHog OSS-tier only ‚Äî unchanged from PR #10).
- **No hard-floor item 6 triggers.** All findings within-scope correctness, privacy, or ergonomic. IAuthProvider (Bucket A ratified) defined the sub-decision space. No ratifier escalation needed.

**Bucket B activation status.** ‚úÖ Code-side COMPLETE end-to-end. Operators can now activate the full sign-in flow by:
1. Provisioning a Supabase project (cf. `BUCKET_B_CREDENTIALS.md` step B1+B2).
2. Setting `COCKPIT_AUTH_PROVIDER=supabase` + `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_*` env vars.
3. Creating an operator user in Supabase Auth.
4. Granting the role via the SQL editor: `UPDATE auth.users SET raw_app_meta_data = COALESCE(raw_app_meta_data, '{}'::jsonb) || jsonb_build_object('roles', ARRAY['operator']::text[]) WHERE email = '<operator-email>';`
5. Running the canonical_events migration on the project.

**Next critical-path items.**
- **Bucket B activation** (Evans's credential provisioning per `BUCKET_B_CREDENTIALS.md`).
- **Bucket C** (ratifier-ceremony-gated): Crisis Response exactly-once audit primitive, `crisis_initiator` role model, Mode 2 case-prep clinical-anchor. Track 6 spec-corpus ratification required before implementation.
- **Day-3+ follow-on**: ingestion worker that tails the telecheckONE canonical_events NDJSON files + writes to the Supabase canonical_events table (telecheckONE-side; out of cockpit scope).

‚Äî Claude (`claude-opus-4-7`, Evans's local session, 2026-05-24), telecheck-cockpit Bucket B continuation SHIPPED on main (PR #11 ‚Üí `2f8b8ec`); 6-round Codex structural lockdown of the authorization-decision surface; final contract = single-path role-claim enforced at middleware + SupabaseAuthProvider + Postgres RLS identically; 46 vitest tests + tsc + next build clean; canonical_events Supabase migration ships RLS that requires `app_metadata.roles ? 'operator'` (matching the app gate exactly). progress.json revision 234 ‚Üí 235.

## Addendum 132 ‚Äî telecheck-cockpit Google OAuth as the canonical sign-in flow SHIPPED on main (PR #12 merged `7201245`); 2-round Codex cycle; email+password retained as dev-only fallback with production hard-stop

**Context.** Per Evans's directive 2026-05-24 (immediately after PR #11 merged), the cockpit's sign-in flow shifts from email+password to **Google OAuth** as the canonical path. Rationale (recommended in the conversation): the cockpit is an operator dashboard tied to a single Google Workspace (`cardinalfive.com`); inheriting Google identity gives MFA + security keys + domain restriction + no stored passwords ‚Äî all for free. Email+password remains available as a collapsed `<details>`-rendered dev-only fallback gated by `NEXT_PUBLIC_COCKPIT_ENABLE_PASSWORD_AUTH=1`.

**What shipped (PR #12 ‚Üí squash `7201245` on `arthurmenson/telecheck-cockpit`, 3 files / +285 / ‚àí76).**

- **`src/app/sign-in/SignInForm.tsx`**: rewritten. Primary button calls `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'google', options: { redirectTo, queryParams: { prompt: 'select_account' }}})`. `redirectTo` is `window.location.origin + /auth/callback?next=...` with the same `/`-prefix open-redirect defense as PR #11's password path. `prompt: 'select_account'` forces Google's account-chooser display so multi-account operators don't auto-sign-in to the wrong account on a shared machine. Multi-colored Google "G" SVG inline (per Google brand guidelines). Email+password form is collapsed under `<details>` and only renders when `NODE_ENV !== "production" && NEXT_PUBLIC_COCKPIT_ENABLE_PASSWORD_AUTH === "1"` ‚Äî production builds hard-disable the form regardless of the env flag (Codex R1 catch: a Vercel typo on the flag could otherwise silently re-enable the weaker auth path). New `ERROR_MESSAGES.oauth_failed` for the OAuth-specific failure mode.

- **`.env.example`**: documented `NEXT_PUBLIC_COCKPIT_ENABLE_PASSWORD_AUTH` with the production hard-stop note + the Google OAuth setup pointer.

- **`BUCKET_B_CREDENTIALS.md`** Step 6 rewritten end-to-end. The 2-hop redirect chain (cockpit ‚Üí Google ‚Üí Supabase ‚Üí cockpit) is now explicitly explained, including the most-common Google-OAuth-with-Supabase setup mistake: mixing up which dashboard owns which allowlist. **Google Cloud Console gets ONLY `https://<ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/callback`** (Google never redirects to the cockpit directly). **Supabase Dashboard ‚Üí Authentication ‚Üí URL Configuration owns the cockpit allowlist** (Site URL + Additional Redirect URLs for production / localhost / Vercel preview wildcard). The Workspace-domain restriction is documented as the recommended defense-in-depth on top of the `operator` role claim.

**Codex cycle (2 rounds ‚Äî tight).**

| Round | Findings | Closure |
|---|---|---|
| R1 | (1) `NEXT_PUBLIC_COCKPIT_ENABLE_PASSWORD_AUTH` could silently re-enable email+password in production via a Vercel typo. (2) The OAuth setup walk-through told operators to add the cockpit's `/auth/callback` to Google Cloud Console ‚Äî that's wrong; Google only knows about the Supabase callback. | `4bca8e7` |
| R-verify | "Keep the NODE_ENV gate" + "Keep the Google Cloud Console instructions; the changed docs correctly say Google should only know the Supabase /auth/v1/callback URI" ‚Äî both closures confirmed ‚Üí **APPROVE** | merged |

Significantly faster than PR #11's 6 rounds ‚Äî most of PR #11's cycle was spent converging on the underlying auth-decision contract (single-path role-claim), which is now stable + unchanged by this PR. R1 here caught two genuinely-novel surfaces specific to OAuth setup that wouldn't have been flagged otherwise.

**Final state.**
- vitest: 46/46 passing (unchanged ‚Äî auth-flow code-paths untouched; only the client-side button wiring + ERROR_MESSAGES dictionary changed).
- tsc: clean.
- next build: clean (9 routes + middleware).
- main = `7201245` on `arthurmenson/telecheck-cockpit`.

**Discipline anchors.**
- **Defense in depth on the password-flag**: even with the env var set on Vercel, production builds refuse to render the form. `NODE_ENV` is inlined at build time by Next.js, so the gate is unsidesteppable post-deploy.
- **Honest setup docs**: the 2-hop redirect-allowlist split was the most common Google-OAuth-with-Supabase mistake and would have stranded Evans during credential provisioning. The corrected docs preempt it.
- **Single-path role-claim contract preserved**: PR #12 only changes how a fresh JWT gets *issued*. The role-claim authorization gate (middleware + SupabaseAuthProvider + Postgres RLS) is unchanged.

**Bucket B activation status (post-#12).** ‚úÖ All cockpit code paths ready. To activate end-to-end:
1. Provision Supabase project (cf. `BUCKET_B_CREDENTIALS.md` Steps B1-B5).
2. Enable Google OAuth in Supabase + create OAuth client in Google Cloud Console (Step 6, two dashboards).
3. Sign in once via the cockpit `/sign-in` page to create your Supabase user.
4. Grant the role: `UPDATE auth.users SET raw_app_meta_data = COALESCE(raw_app_meta_data, '{}'::jsonb) || jsonb_build_object('roles', ARRAY['operator']::text[]) WHERE email = '<your-email>';` (Step 8).
5. Run canonical_events migration.

‚Äî Claude (`claude-opus-4-7`, Evans's local session, 2026-05-24), telecheck-cockpit Google OAuth as canonical sign-in SHIPPED on main (PR #12 ‚Üí `7201245`); 2-round Codex cycle (R1 caught production-hard-stop gap + OAuth redirect-allowlist-split-misconfig ‚Üí R-verify APPROVE); email+password retained as dev-only fallback with production hard-stop; BUCKET_B_CREDENTIALS.md Step 6 now correctly splits the 2-hop redirect-URI allowlist between Google Cloud Console (hop 1 ‚Üí Supabase) and Supabase Dashboard URL Configuration (hop 2 ‚Üí cockpit); single-path role-claim authorization contract from PR #11 preserved. progress.json revision 235 ‚Üí 236.

## Addendum 133 ‚Äî remote-cron firing: 4th-consecutive parked-state re-verification; every named pilot-viable priority is DONE, `[CODEX-PENDING]`-queued, or ratification-gated; no non-duplicative buildable PR exists in this env; loop-pacing recommendation surfaced

This is the **4th consecutive firing** to land on the parked state (cf. Addenda 127/128/129-130).

| Premise | Method | Result |
|---|---|---|
| Codex availability | `$OPENAI_API_KEY` + `which codex` + `~/.claude/plugins/cache/openai-codex` | **UNAVAILABLE** ‚Äî key UNSET, no bin, no plugin |

| SI ratification | PR queue | SI-003/004/005 still pre-ratification (#137/#138/#139 open) |

**Incremental contribution this firing ‚Äî full tally of the 5 named pilot-viable priorities against *actual repo state* (not against prior addenda's claims):**

1. **(a) Med-Interaction DB layer** ‚Äî DONE. Migrations `046`‚Äì`050` (rbac_roles, entities, view/MV-access fn, raw-lifecycle writer, wrappers) all present on main; rollbacks `046`‚Äì`050` present. Write handlers are the queued #208 (PR 8) + #209 (PR 9, stacked).
2. **(b) Async-Consult clinician decision loop** ‚Äî RATIFICATION-GATED on SI-005 (`Telecheck_Consult_Schema`), still pre-ratification (#139). Cannot implement ahead of spec per "ratification leads implementation by ‚â•1 sprint."
3. **(c) AI Service Mode 1 chat handler** ‚Äî DONE. `POST /v0/ai/chat` **mounted 2026-05-15** (`src/modules/ai-service/internal/handlers/chat.ts`; routes.ts `mode_1_chat_handler_mounted: true`): full lifecycle wired ‚Äî I-019 input crisis gate (Cat A) ‚Üí Conservative-Default guardrail ‚Üí NullLLMProvider (AI-RESIL-001 fail-soft) ‚Üí FLOOR-020 Cat C response audit. NOT a gap. (Mode 2 case-prep is the queued #210, gated on protocol-engine/I-012.)
4. **(d) Crisis Response Sprint 2 handlers** ‚Äî all queued: initiate (#201) already merged to main; acknowledge (#199), respond+resolve (#202), patient-summary read (#203), sweep (#204) queued.
5. **(e) Admin Backend Sprint 2 handlers** ‚Äî submit (#205) merged; review-decision (#206), dashboard-reads (#207) queued.

**Conclusion: there is no un-queued, non-duplicative, ratified, buildable PR available in this environment.** Every named priority is DONE, sitting in the `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue (re-authoring = forbidden duplication), or ratification-gated (implementing ahead = forbidden). Authoring a "next" Sprint-3 PR would stack on un-merged queue PRs (a dependency chain on an undrainable queue = fragile-rebase make-work). Per Addendum 130's explicit finding, re-running the 9-branch `merge-tree` triage on an **unmoved** main is guaranteed-identical and is itself forbidden make-work ‚Äî so it was deliberately NOT re-run this firing.

**Loop-pacing recommendation (surfaced to Evans ‚Äî single-reviewer; the dual-recommendation Codex consult is structurally impossible here because Codex is unavailable in this env).** The remote-cron loop has now fired into the identical parked state 4√ó consecutively, each firing a no-op-by-correct-discipline. The honest recommendation is one of: (1) **drain the 9-PR queue** in a Codex-enabled local session ‚Äî recommended order #199‚Üí#202‚Üí#203‚Üí#204, #206‚Üí#207, #208‚Üí#209, #210; OR (2) **run the CDM v1.2‚Üív1.3 + SI-003/004/005 ratification ceremony** (the highest-leverage unblock ‚Äî opens Tracks 1/2/3 net-new work); OR (3) **pause the remote-cron loop** until (1) or (2) lands, to stop spending firings on no-op triages. The loop cannot self-unblock ‚Äî both barriers are Evans-gated.

**Stale-tracking-ref trap (8th consecutive).** telecheckONE local `main` label lagged at stale `6adaae5` (Addendum 71 / r175 era) while detached clone HEAD + `git ls-remote` both reported true tip `6f6f314` (Addendum 132 / r236); realigned via `git checkout -B main HEAD` + `git fetch origin main` (which force-updated the stale `origin/main` tracking ref `6adaae5`‚Üí`6f6f314`) before appending. telecheck-app local `main` label was in sync (`c27638c`) this firing. Lesson re-reinforced: trust `git ls-remote`/HEAD, never the local branch label, on a fresh container.

**No code branch created; telecheck-app main untouched by code this firing.** This Addendum + the progress bump are the only artifacts.

## Addendum 135 ‚Äî telecheck-cockpit OperatorChip (Topbar operator identity + sign-out menu) SHIPPED on main (PR #13 merged `d59fc61`); closes the Bucket B activation UX gap from PRs #11/#12 with a 4-round Codex cycle

**Context.** PRs #11 and #12 shipped the cockpit's `/auth/sign-out` route + Google OAuth sign-in flow, but there was no UI affordance for the operator to invoke sign-out + no surface telling them which account was currently signed in. PR #13 closes both gaps with a Topbar chip showing the operator's identity + a popover menu hosting the sign-out form. Pure cockpit UX work ‚Äî no spec corpus changes, no auth-decision-contract changes.

**What shipped (PR #13 ‚Üí squash `d59fc61` on `arthurmenson/telecheck-cockpit`, 5 files / +488 / ‚àí5).**

- **`src/components/shell/OperatorChip.tsx`** (new): client component reading `/api/auth/status`. Five explicit render states:
  - **loading** ‚Äî skeleton ("‚Ä¶") during the initial fetch.
  - **authenticated** ‚Äî initials-avatar + display name + ‚ñæ caret. Click opens a popover with full identity (id, roles[]), an amber "Local dev session" banner pointing at BUCKET_B_CREDENTIALS.md when `provider === "null"`, and a sign-out form POSTing to `/auth/sign-out`.
  - **unauthenticated** (`reason: "unauthenticated"`) ‚Äî "Sign in" link to `/sign-in`. Reasonable fallback when the operator landed without a session.
  - **forbidden** (`reason: "forbidden"`) ‚Äî amber-bordered "Not authorized ¬∑ Sign out" form button. The user HAS a valid session but lacks the `operator` role claim; sending them to /sign-in would just reproduce the forbidden state. Hover-title points at the BUCKET_B_CREDENTIALS.md step 7 recovery (set `app_metadata.roles` via SQL ‚Üí sign out + back in).
  - **provider_unavailable** (`reason: "provider_unavailable"` OR fetch error) ‚Äî red-dot "auth offline" honest-failure indicator. Backend is unreachable; the recovery is operator-config, not a sign-in retry. Never silently renders a "signed in" fiction.
- **`/api/auth/status` route** amended: response now carries a `reason: "unauthenticated" | "forbidden" | "provider_unavailable"` discriminator in the not-authenticated case. Prior shape collapsed `provider_unavailable` into `authenticated:false + actor:null` with no way to distinguish from a genuine "no session", which would have routed the operator into a /sign-in retry that fails identically.
- **Topbar wiring**: `<OperatorChip />` appended after the theme toggle at the right edge of the Topbar.
- **Test infra**: `vitest.setup.ts` (new) + `vitest.config.ts` amended with `setupFiles` to register `@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest` matchers globally (so component tests can use `toBeInTheDocument`, `toHaveAttribute`, etc. without per-file imports). Benefits future component tests too.
- **Tests**: 13 new vitest cases across `OperatorChip.test.tsx` + `SignInForm.test.tsx`. Coverage spans every render state, popover open/close + outside-click dismissal, sign-out form action assertion, honest-failure on fetch reject + HTTP error, NullAuthProvider banner, AND reachable-flow coverage of `/sign-in?error=not_authorized` + `?error=oauth_failed` recovery copy (regression against the role-grant-path messaging that landed in PRs #11/#12).

**Codex cycle (4 rounds).**

| Round | Findings | Closure SHA |
|---|---|---|
| R1 | (1) `/api/auth/status` collapsed `provider_unavailable` into `authenticated:false`, causing the chip to show a misleading "Sign in" link when the auth backend was unreachable. (2) Chip subscribed to `cockpit:refresh` (5s polling) ‚Üí 12 hits/min/tab on Supabase for no useful signal. | `37b4169` |
| R-verify | The `reason: "forbidden"` case fell through to the same Sign-in-link branch as `unauthenticated`, but a forbidden user has a valid session ‚Äî sending them to /sign-in reproduces the state. | `052c942` |
| R-verify-2 | Recommended adding a reachable-flow regression around `/sign-in?error=not_authorized` to lock in the role-grant-path recovery copy. | `8c6faee` |
| R-verify-3 | **APPROVE no material findings.** "The forbidden chip state now renders a POST sign-out form instead of a sign-in link, and the new SignInForm regression pins the /sign-in?error=not_authorized recovery copy to the actual role-grant path." | merged |

The trajectory pattern was Codex finding under-specified surface in the auth-status response shape (R1) then progressively tightening the chip's render-state matrix (R-verify, R-verify-2). The architectural fix landed in R1's `reason` discriminator addition ‚Äî every subsequent round was applying that discriminator to a missed branch or adding regression coverage. R-verify-3 returned a clean explicit APPROVE.

**Final state.**
- vitest: 59/59 passing (up from 46 at PR open; +13 tests covering OperatorChip render states + SignInForm error-message regression).
- tsc: clean.
- next build: clean (10 routes + middleware including the new chip's dependencies through Topbar).
- main = `d59fc61` on `arthurmenson/telecheck-cockpit`.

**Discipline anchors.**
- **Honest failure preserved** ‚Äî every render state distinguishes "operator action required" from "backend broken." No silently-misleading affordance.
- **Single-path authorization contract preserved** ‚Äî chip is a CONSUMER of `/api/auth/status`; it doesn't make auth decisions itself. The PR #11/#12 single-path role-claim contract (`app_metadata.roles ? 'operator'`) is untouched.
- **No new env vars / no schema changes / no platform-floor amendments.** Pure UX surface.
- **Test infra improvement (`vitest.setup.ts`)** ‚Äî global jest-dom matchers will pay off on every future component test. Was a Day-2 obligation that finally got closed under this PR's scope.

**Bucket B cockpit activation surface ‚Äî status.** Code-side COMPLETE. The UX gap from PRs #11/#12 (no sign-out button, no operator-identity surface) is now closed. To activate:
1. Provision Supabase + Google OAuth (per `BUCKET_B_CREDENTIALS.md`).
2. Sign in once via cockpit `/sign-in` ‚Üí creates Supabase user.
3. Run the SQL from step 8 to grant the user `app_metadata.roles: ["operator"]`.
4. Sign out + back in to pick up the role claim.
5. OperatorChip should now show your initials + email; click ‚Üí sign-out menu available.

**Next critical-path items.**
- **Bucket B activation** (Evans's credential provisioning): unchanged from prior Addenda.
- **ChatScreen ‚Üí real Anthropic API smoke** (cockpit follow-on): the chat backend reads `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (present in your shell env); end-to-end smoke vs. the fake-orchestrator fallback hasn't been exercised since PR #8.
- **MJML / HTML email templates** (per Addendum-conversation today): parked as open sub-decision. Email vendor (Postmark) is in the canonical stack; authoring language is unprescribed.
- **Bucket C** (ratifier-ceremony-gated): unchanged.

‚Äî Claude (`claude-opus-4-7`, Evans's local session, 2026-05-24), telecheck-cockpit OperatorChip + sign-out menu SHIPPED on main (PR #13 ‚Üí `d59fc61`); 4-round Codex cycle (R1 provider_unavailable + 5s spam ‚Üí R-verify forbidden branch ‚Üí R-verify-2 regression coverage ‚Üí R-verify-3 APPROVE); 59 vitest tests + tsc + next build clean; Topbar now surfaces operator identity + sign-out without leaving the cockpit; vitest.setup.ts global jest-dom matchers retire a Day-2 obligation. progress.json revision 238 ‚Üí 239.

## Addendum 139 ‚Äî telecheck-cockpit SpecCorpus canonical-versions surface STRUCTURALLY HARDENED on main (PR #14 merged `69c2230`); auto-discovery + max-across-family + three-state provenance + UI badge + 19 regression tests after 7-round Codex cycle

**Context.** Item 1 of the post-Bucket-B cockpit work plan was a live-data smoke of every cockpit screen. The smoke surfaced 3 stale-ahead version labels on the SpecCorpus screen (CDM v1.5 ‚Üí on-disk v1.2, Contracts Pack v5.3 ‚Üí v5.2, Artifact Registry v2.11 ‚Üí v2.10). The 1-line stale-label patch evolved into a structural overhaul of the canonical-version surface across 7 Codex verify rounds.

**What shipped (PR #14 ‚Üí squash `69c2230` on `arthurmenson/telecheck-cockpit`, 5 files / +532 / ‚àí44; 79 vitest tests passing).**

- **Header auto-discovery.** New helpers `readBundleVersion()` + `parseVersionFromHeader()` in `src/lib/server/load-cockpit-state.ts` read each canonical-bundle file's `**Version:**` header at load time. No more hardcoded version labels ‚Äî ratifier ceremony amendments to file headers bump the cockpit surface automatically.
- **Max-across-family resolution for wildcard paths.** Contracts Pack family (`Telecheck_Contracts_Pack_v5_00_*.md`) ‚Äî files in the family don't always declare the same version (P-027 Phase B amended INVARIANTS to v5.3 + some files reached v5.4). The loader reads ALL matching files + returns the MAX header version; that's the authoritative state across the family. (Codex R-verify catch ‚Äî original implementation only read the first-alphabetical file.)
- **Three-state provenance discriminator** (`versionSource: "header" | "filename" | "missing"`). Added to `CanonicalVersion` in `src/lib/cockpit-types.ts`. The loader marks each entry by how the version was obtained ‚Äî operators can distinguish header-derived (authoritative) from filename-fallback (degraded) from missing-on-disk (most-degraded). Codex R-verify-2 catch: prior version presented fallback values as if they were canonical truth.
- **Visible UI provenance badge** on the SpecCorpusScreen canonical-versions table. Header-derived entries render plainly; `filename` entries get an amber "UNVERIFIED" badge with hover-title; `missing` entries get an amber "MISSING" badge. `aria-label="provenance: {filename,missing}"` for screen readers. Codex R-verify-3 catch: API discriminator was useless if UI didn't surface it.
- **Hardened version-grammar regex** with word-boundary defense + same-line whitespace lockdown + patch-level support:
  - `(?!\w)` lookahead after `**Version:**` rejects longer labels (`**Versioned:**`, `**Version:Number**`). R-verify-4.
  - `[ \t]*` (not `\s*`) requires same-line whitespace ‚Äî `**Version:**\n1.0` no longer mis-parses. R-verify-6.
  - `(?:\.\d+){0,2}` supports MAJOR, MAJOR.MINOR, MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH (vital for the v1.10.1 hygiene-cycle pattern). R-verify-4.
  - `versionRank()` encoding (major √ó 1e6 + minor √ó 1e3 + patch) ‚Äî strictly orders v1.10.1 > v1.10, v2.0 > v1.99. R-verify-5.
- **Strict guard tests** (`src/lib/server/load-cockpit-state.test.ts` ‚Äî new file). No silent-skip on missing files. Asserts every entry resolves to a real header-parseable file with `versionSource === "header"`. Catches any future drift (label, file-rename, header-format-change, file-removal).
- **UI test for the provenance badge** (`src/components/screens/SpecCorpusScreen.test.tsx` ‚Äî new file). 3 vitest cases pin the visible "UNVERIFIED" / "MISSING" badge behavior across all three versionSource states.
- **19 grammar + provenance regression tests** total: bare integer-minor, v-prefixed minor, patch-level (v1.10.1 + v5.3.0), Contracts Pack inline mid-line format, null on no header, no-match on longer labels, malformed continuations (pre-release suffixes, empty, non-version-shaped, newline-bridging), same-line-only whitespace (LF / CRLF / tab-then-LF rejection, tab acceptance), shape regex tolerance for patch-level, ranking order.

**Codex cycle ‚Äî the longest in the cockpit's lifecycle to date (7 verify rounds).**

| Round | Catch | Closure |
|---|---|---|
| R1 | Header-auto-discovery recommendation + guard test recommendation | `67c28f9` (guard test only) |
| R-verify | Tighten wildcard guard (forced full auto-discovery) | `88f6626` (auto-discovery + max-across-family) |
| R-verify-2 | Silent-skip on missing files + no fallback provenance | `67a0726` (versionSource + strict guard) |
| R-verify-3 | versionSource not surfaced to UI + no UI test | `ba4bc1b` (provenance badge + UI tests) |
| R-verify-4 | Parser boundary + patch-level versions | `5db6ce3` (grammar fix + 7 regression tests) |
| R-verify-5 | Shape guard regex too strict; need negative-continuation tests | `4a69430` (relax shape regex + 4 negative tests) |
| R-verify-6 | `\s*` bridges LF/CRLF ‚Äî same-line whitespace lockdown | `8cbc3f3` (`[ \t]*` + 4 LF/CRLF tests) |
| R-verify-7 | _(would have come ‚Äî Evans authorized merge given diminishing-returns trajectory)_ | merged |

The trajectory: every round found ONE more real edge case (each technically correct), getting progressively smaller ‚Äî LF vs CRLF vs tab-then-LF in regex grammar by the end. The visible operator-state outcome (correct on-disk header versions surfaced on the SpecCorpus screen) was achieved at R-verify-2; rounds 3-6 hardened the grammar + tests around that outcome. Evans authorized merge at R-verify-7 territory: "the structural surface is hardened well past what the original 1-line stale-label catch warranted."

**Discovered actual canonical state (cockpit live state today).** Auto-discovery shows the FILE-HEADER versions, which differ from the project CLAUDE.md "Current canonical versions" table in three places:

| Artifact | CLAUDE.md says | File header declares | Filename |
|---|---|---|---|
| Master PRD | v1.10 | v1.10 | v1_10 |
| **CDM** | v1.2 | **v1.4** | v1_2 |
| OpenAPI | v0.2 | v0.2 | v0_2 |
| **State Machines** | v1.1 | **v1.2** | v1_1 |
| RBAC | v1.1 | v1.1 | v1_1 |
| **Contracts Pack** | v5.2 | **v5.4** (MAX across family) | v5_00_* (intra-family v5.2 / v5.3 / v5.4) |
| **Artifact Registry** | v2.10 | **v2.29** | v2_10 |

This drift is the spec-corpus-side action item Evans flagged ("canonicalise the highest versions for spec corpus") ‚Äî a separate Promotion Ledger entry will reconcile the CLAUDE.md table to the file-header reality.

**Final state.**
- vitest: 79/79 passing (61 ‚Üí 79; +18 grammar/provenance/UI regression tests).
- tsc: clean.
- next build: clean.
- main = `69c2230` on `arthurmenson/telecheck-cockpit`.

**Discipline anchors.**
- **Structural fix completed iterative paint-fix.** A 1-line stale-label patch could have been merged at R1 ("just change the numbers"). Codex's R-verify catches forced the structural realization that hardcoded labels are the wrong shape ‚Äî auto-discovery + provenance + visible UI affordance is the right shape. The 7-round cost was justified by the resulting structural surface.
- **Diminishing returns recognized + merged.** R-verify-6 ‚Üí R-verify-7 territory was finding incrementally smaller edge cases. Evans's "merge" authorization at that point reflects the rule "ship when the structural surface is sound + remaining catches are incremental tooling polish."
- **Live-state outcome preserved.** The visible operator-state ‚Äî correct on-disk header versions on the SpecCorpus screen ‚Äî was achieved at R-verify-2 (commit `67a0726`). Subsequent rounds hardened the underlying grammar + tests but didn't change what operators see.
- **No hard-floor item 6 triggers.** All findings were within-scope correctness, parsing grammar, or test discipline ‚Äî none proposed net-new schema, invariants, or platform-floor primitives. No ratifier escalation needed.

**Bonus memory artifact.** During the smoke that surfaced this PR, hit a Python-stdin cp1252 false-mojibake debugging detour (~45 min). The cockpit was producing correct UTF-8 on the wire; `python -c "json.load(sys.stdin)"` was decoding as Windows-1252. Saved to memory as `reference_python_stdin_cp1252.md` ‚Äî future sessions know to read `sys.stdin.buffer.read().decode("utf-8")` explicitly.

Awaiting Evans's clarification on (a) unilateral ratifier authorization vs. ERR ceremony, and (b) Contracts Pack intra-family inconsistency resolution shape.

**Incremental contribution this firing ‚Äî the #209 rebase Addendum 138 deferred to Evans, executed + a DEEPER CI-red repair than #204.** Addendum 138 flagged #209 `mergeable_state=behind` (head `62fe013` off stale base `f6c5160`, with a RED `Build, lint, typecheck, test` job) and recommended Evans rebase before the cascade. This firing did it:

1. **Rebased** `feat/med-interaction-pr9-remaining-write-handlers` onto current main `c27638c` ‚Äî clean, no conflicts (#209's 8 files are all net-new med-interaction module files; zero overlap with main's CI-fix wave). The rebase alone cleared `behind`.
2. **Diagnosed the red in a CI-identical PG16 cluster** (initdb + `telecheck_ci`/`postgres` roles + `telecheck_test` DB + full ci.yml env block incl. `REDIS_URL`/`TENANT_KMS_LOCAL_DEV_KEY`/`TELECHECK_SPEC_PATH`). Unlike #204 (prettier-only), #209's red had **three layers**, the gate-1 format failure masking the rest:
   - **format:check** ‚Äî 6 new files (`audit.ts` + 4 handlers + pr9 test) committed unformatted. (Same class as #204.)
   - **lint** ‚Äî `import/order` (15, auto-fixed) + **`@typescript-eslint/no-floating-promises` (11)**: the `reply.code(...).send(...)` calls in each handler's `mapServiceError` omitted the `void` operator that the canonical pattern uses across ai-service / identity / async-consult / admin-backend. Prepended `void` (lint-compliance only ‚Äî `.send()` still fires, `return true` unchanged; **handler behavior untouched**).
   - **test fixtures (the substantive find)** ‚Äî the `VALID_*` ULID constants in `pr9-write-handlers.test.ts` contained **Crockford-forbidden letters** (I/L/O/U from `SIGNAL`/`EVALUATION`/`CLINICIAN`/`DISCONT`/`IDEMPOTENCY`) and one was 27 chars, so **all 10 pr9 tests failed path-validation before reaching the asserted behavior**. CI never saw this because it bailed at gate-1 format. Replaced with valid 26-char Crockford base32 ULIDs. The tests are well-written and assert correct behavior (path-before-body validation order; I-003 audit-on-rejection for the fail-closed paths); they just had invalid fixture literals. **Handlers untouched.**
3. **Verified** locally: format + lint + typecheck + build clean; med-interaction 26/26; **full suite 2035 passed | 3 skipped | 30 todo (2068 total)** ‚Äî exceeds Addendum 138's 2058 bar (count grew because #209 adds tests). Committed the repair as a separate commit on top of the rebased original (`ef5c27c ‚Üí 2b5c48a`, mirroring #204's separate-fix-commit pattern) and force-pushed with `--force-with-lease` (lease matched remote `62fe013`; nothing unexpected clobbered). **#209 stays `[CODEX-PENDING]` ‚Äî NOT merged** (Codex unavailable; discipline floor). PR head now `2b5c48a`, base `c27638c`, `mergeable_state` moved `behind ‚Üí blocked` (the normal awaiting-required-checks state). **CI on `2b5c48a`: all 4 checks GREEN ‚Äî verify-metadata + benchmarks + dependency-review + `Build, lint, typecheck, test` (the previously-red job) all `success` (build job completed 01:34 UTC), authoritatively confirming the repair on the actual CI runner, not just the local cluster.**

**Candor note (queue-premise correction).** The 9-PR queue's working premise ‚Äî "authored, just needs Codex review + merge" ‚Äî was **partially false for #209**: it was placed in the queue in a state that was never green (format + lint + 10 failing tests). This is a scope-class deeper than #204's cosmetic-only red and worth flagging for whoever drains the cascade: **#209's repair edited test FIXTURES, not just whitespace.** The edits are mechanical and zero-behavior-change (handlers untouched; the now-valid fixtures make the existing tests exercise the paths they always intended), and Codex will review the full diff before any merge ‚Äî but the queue should not be assumed uniformly green-on-arrival. Recommend a spot-check of the remaining un-probed queue members (#199/#202/#203/#206/#207/#208/#210) for the same format/lint/fixture-validity class before the cascade, since #204 and #209 both shipped with gate-1 format red and #209 additionally with invalid fixtures.

**Conclusion: there is still no un-queued, non-duplicative, ratified, buildable+mergeable PR available in this environment** ‚Äî every pilot-viable priority (a)-(e) maps 1:1 onto a queue PR or merged commit (unchanged from Addenda 137/138). The single net-new, non-Codex-gated, non-duplicative action available this firing was finishing the #209 rebase-and-repair toward drainable-clean, which is done. Codex unavailable ‚Üí nothing merges from here regardless.

**Loop-pacing recommendation ‚Äî held at PAUSE.** 9th consecutive Evans-gated parked firing; the loop retains zero self-unblock levers for merging. Priority-ordered actions unchanged: (1) **PAUSE the remote-cron loop** until (2) or (3) lands; (2) **drain the 9-PR `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue** in a Codex-enabled local session ‚Äî now with both #204 and #209 confirmed drainable-clean (the two PRs prior firings flagged), the full queue is to-the-best-of-this-firing's-knowledge mergeable-clean; (3) **run the CDM + SI-003/004/005 + Plan-v1.1 batched ratifier ceremony** (charter STOP condition ‚Äî Evans + Engineering Lead + CDM owner).

**Incremental contribution this firing ‚Äî caught + corrected a NEW variant of the stale-ref trap, in telecheckONE itself.** CLAUDE.md's documented trap is telecheck-app's local `origin/main` *tracking ref* lagging the live remote. This firing surfaced a sharper variant in the **spec repo**: the container started with `HEAD` detached at the true remote tip `99b6375` (Addendum 141 / progress 245 / P-044 CLAUDE.md), but the local **`main` branch ref itself** ‚Äî and its `origin/main` tracking ref ‚Äî both lagged at `6adaae5` (pre-P-044 / progress 175, i.e. before the P-044 canonical-versions ratification AND Addendum 141). A routine `git checkout main` therefore **silently reverted the working tree** from the correct detached-HEAD content to the stale branch content (`git status` reported "clean" + "up to date with origin/main" the whole time, because both local refs agreed with each other ‚Äî just not with the live remote). Caught via a content cross-check: `progress.json` flipped 245 ‚Üí 175 and CLAUDE.md's canonical-versions table reverted P-044 ‚Üí pre-P-044 across the `checkout`. Corrected per CLAUDE.md guidance ‚Äî `git ls-remote` (authoritative) confirmed `99b6375`; `git fetch origin main` + `git reset --hard origin/main` restored the worktree to the true tip before any edit landed. **Lesson for the next firing: in telecheckONE, do not trust `git checkout main` to land on current state ‚Äî verify `git rev-parse main` against `git ls-remote origin refs/heads/main` and fetch+reset first, exactly as for telecheck-app.** No spec content was lost; the catch happened before any write.

**No net-new PR authored this firing ‚Äî deliberately.** With the queue confirmed intact + spot-checked green and every priority (a)-(e) mapping 1:1 onto a queued PR or merged commit, there is no un-queued, non-duplicative, ratified, buildable item to author, and no red to repair. Authoring or re-touching any queue member would be make-work and/or duplication ‚Äî forbidden by the discipline floor.

**Conclusion: still no un-queued, non-duplicative, ratified, buildable+mergeable PR available in this environment**, and Codex unavailable ‚Üí nothing merges from here regardless. The loop retains zero self-unblock levers for merging.

**Loop-pacing recommendation ‚Äî held at PAUSE.** 11th consecutive Evans-gated parked firing. Priority-ordered operator actions (all human-gated; unchanged from Addenda 140/141):
1. **PAUSE the remote-cron loop** until (2) or (3) lands ‚Äî continued firings can only re-verify, not advance, and risk make-work drift. (The marginal value of each additional parked firing is now near-zero; the strongest case for any continued firing is regression-detection on the queue, which a much slower cadence would still cover.)
2. **Drain the 9-PR `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue** in a Codex-enabled local session ‚Äî directly-verified CI-green-on-HEAD (Addendum 141 full-9 + this firing's #208/#210 spot-check) and based on current main; nothing blocks the Codex‚Üímerge cascade but Codex availability.
3. **Run the CDM + SI-003/004/005 + Plan-v1.1 batched ratifier ceremony** (charter STOP condition ‚Äî Evans + Engineering Lead + CDM owner).

**Code pushed this firing: NONE** (no telecheck-app branch/commit ‚Äî nothing to author or repair; queue confirmed intact + green). telecheck-app main untouched (`c27638c`). This Addendum + the `progress.json` revision bump (telecheckONE repo) are the only artifacts.

‚Äî Claude (`claude-opus-4-7`, remote-cron autonomous firing ‚Äî Codex unavailable in this env), 2026-05-25. 11th-consecutive parked-state firing. | progress.json | telecheckONE (NOT telecheck-app ‚Äî brief path stale) | revision **246** (Addendum 142) ‚Üí bumped to **247** this firing. |

**Incremental contribution this firing ‚Äî the telecheckONE stale-ref trap is now confirmed PERSISTENT, not a one-off.** Addendum 142 caught and corrected a sharp variant of the stale-ref trap *inside the spec repo* (local `main` branch ref + its `origin/main` tracking ref both lagging the live remote, so `git checkout main` silently reverts the worktree). The open question that left was whether that was a transient artifact of one container or a standing hazard. This firing answers it: a **full firing later**, the container *again* provisioned with `HEAD` detached at the true remote tip (now `aa05fbb7` = Addendum 142's commit) while local `main` AND `origin/main` *both again* sit at the identical stale `6adaae5` (progress-175 / pre-P-044 era). So the trap is **deterministic container-provisioning behavior in telecheckONE, not a fluke** ‚Äî every future firing must treat it as standing. Handled correctly this firing: stayed on the true-tip detached HEAD (verified `git rev-parse HEAD` == `git ls-remote origin refs/heads/main` == `aa05fbb7`), edited the worktree in place, and will land `main` via `git checkout -B main HEAD` (force main to the verified true tip) rather than `git checkout main` (which would revert). No content cross-check flip this firing because the worktree was never reverted ‚Äî the lesson from Addendum 142 was applied pre-emptively. **Standing instruction for the next firing, hardened: in telecheckONE NEVER `git checkout main`; verify the detached HEAD against `git ls-remote`, edit in place, and `git checkout -B main HEAD` only after confirming HEAD == live remote tip.**

**No net-new PR authored this firing ‚Äî deliberately.** Every priority (a)-(e) maps 1:1 onto a queued PR or merged commit; re-authoring any is forbidden duplication. The only non-Codex-gated, non-queued candidate class (gate-1 CI-red on a queue member) was retired across Addenda 138 (#204 format) / 140 (#209 format+lint+ULID) and confirmed fully drained by Addendum 141's all-9 green-on-HEAD audit; with telecheck-app main unmoved (`c27638c`), no queue PR can have gone `behind`, and CI cannot regress without a push, so no red exists to repair. Re-running the full all-9 check-runs audit would be guaranteed-identical ‚Üí forbidden make-work per Addendum 130. SI-003/004/005 + CDM + Plan-v1.1 ratification is a charter STOP condition. Dependabot PRs (#214/#190/#189/#91/#90) are dependency-change class (prohibited-action + Codex-gated) and off the pilot-viable critical path.

**Conclusion: still no un-queued, non-duplicative, ratified, buildable+mergeable PR available in this environment**, and Codex unavailable ‚Üí nothing merges from here regardless. The loop retains zero self-unblock levers for merging or for non-make-work authoring.

**Loop-pacing recommendation ‚Äî held at PAUSE.** 12th consecutive Evans-gated parked firing; marginal value of each additional parked firing remains near-zero (its only residual function is regression-detection on the queue, which a far slower cadence covers). Priority-ordered operator actions (all human-gated; unchanged from Addenda 140/141/142):
1. **PAUSE the remote-cron loop** until (2) or (3) lands.
2. **Drain the 9-PR `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue** in a Codex-enabled local session ‚Äî all directly-verified CI-green-on-HEAD (Addendum 141 full-9) and based on current main `c27638c`; nothing blocks the Codex‚Üímerge cascade but Codex availability.
3. **Run the CDM + SI-003/004/005 + Plan-v1.1 batched ratifier ceremony** (charter STOP condition ‚Äî Evans + Engineering Lead + CDM owner).

**Code pushed this firing: NONE** (no telecheck-app branch/commit ‚Äî nothing to author or repair; queue confirmed intact). telecheck-app main untouched (`c27638c`). This Addendum + the `progress.json` revision bump (telecheckONE repo) are the only artifacts.

‚Äî Claude (`claude-opus-4-7`, remote-cron autonomous firing ‚Äî Codex unavailable in this env), 2026-05-25. 12th-consecutive parked-state firing. Re-verified all premises from primary sources: Codex unavailable (key absent, `api.openai.com` 403); telecheck-app main unmoved (`c27638c`); 9-PR `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue intact + all still pending (#199/#202/#203/#204/#206/#207/#208/#209/#210); every priority (a)-(e) covered 1:1; SI-003/004/005 pre-ratification; migration high-water 052. Confirmed the telecheckONE stale-ref trap is PERSISTENT container behavior (local `main`+`origin/main` again at stale `6adaae5` while true tip `aa05fbb7`); handled pre-emptively (stayed on true-tip detached HEAD, `checkout -B main HEAD`). No net-new PR (no buildable/non-duplicative item; authoring = make-work). Loop-pacing held at PAUSE. progress.json revision 246 ‚Üí 247.

**NEW finding this firing ‚Äî the Codex blocker is now actionable, not categorical.** Prior addenda (136‚Äì148) reported the wall as "key absent + no CLI + 403." This firing's probe shows `npx -y @openai/codex` now successfully installs and runs `codex-cli 0.133.0` in this container ‚Äî so "no CLI" is no longer true. The residual blocker is exactly two things, both human/operator-gated: **(1)** no credential (`OPENAI_API_KEY` unset AND not logged in via ChatGPT auth ‚Üí no `~/.codex/auth.json`); **(2)** no network egress to OpenAI (`api.openai.com` ‚Üí 403). If a future container is provisioned with EITHER an `OPENAI_API_KEY` env var OR a logged-in `~/.codex/auth.json`, AND the network policy permits `api.openai.com`, then `OPENAI_API_KEY=$OPENAI_API_KEY npx -y @openai/codex review --base main` (the brief's incantation) becomes runnable and the 9-PR queue can be drained autonomously from a remote firing. This is a sharper unblock spec for Evans than "Codex unavailable."

**No net-new PR authored this firing ‚Äî deliberately.** Every priority (a)-(e) maps 1:1 onto a queued PR or merged commit; re-authoring any is forbidden duplication, and authoring a fresh PR onto the already-9-deep unmergeable `[CODEX-PENDING]` backlog is negative-value make-work (more to review later, zero closer to merge while Codex is walled). The only candidate that isn't ratification-gated or duplicative would be a CI-repair on a queue member, but main is unmoved (`c27638c`) and the queue was directly verified CI-green-on-HEAD at Addendum 141, so no member can have regressed without a push. SI-003/004/005 + CDM + Plan-v1.1 ratification is a charter STOP condition. **telecheck-app stale-ref trap ‚Äî ABSENT this firing.** Local `HEAD` provisioned already at the true remote tip (`git rev-parse HEAD` == `git ls-remote origin refs/heads/main` == **`c27638c`**). Re-authoring any is forbidden duplication; authoring a fresh PR onto the already-9-deep unmergeable `[CODEX-PENDING]` backlog is negative-value make-work (grows the backlog a future Codex session must drain in order; risks stacking-conflict rework). SI-003/004/005 + CDM + Plan-v1.1 ratification is a charter STOP condition. Dependabot PRs (#90/#91/#189/#190/#214) are dependency-change class (Codex-gated + off the pilot-viable critical path). Verified `git rev-parse HEAD` == `git ls-remote origin refs/heads/main` == `47ff1f3`, edited the worktree in place, and will land `main` via `git checkout -B main HEAD` (NOT `git checkout main`, which would silently revert the worktree to stale content) before pushing. (The telecheck-app stale-ref trap was ALSO present this firing ‚Äî local tracking ref lagged at `baca008` ‚Äî and was corrected via `git fetch origin main` + `git reset --hard origin/main` to `c27638c`.)

**Conclusion: still no un-queued, non-duplicative, ratified, buildable+mergeable PR available in this environment**, and Codex cannot run from here (no credential + npx-fetch-incomplete + 403) regardless. The loop retains zero self-unblock levers for merging or for non-make-work authoring.

**Loop-pacing recommendation ‚Äî held at PAUSE.** 24th consecutive Evans-gated parked firing; marginal value of each additional parked firing is near-zero (residual function is queue regression-detection, which a far slower cadence covers). Priority-ordered operator actions (all human-gated):

4. **Run the CDM + SI-003/004/005 + Plan-v1.1 batched ratifier ceremony** (charter STOP condition ‚Äî Evans + Engineering Lead + CDM owner).

**Brief-vs-reality reconciliation (unchanged from Addenda 130‚Äì162).** This firing's brief again carried the same stale premises: (i) "most recent migration is 045" ‚Äî actual high-water is **052** (confirmed `ls migrations/` this firing); (ii) priority (a) "Med-Interaction slice DB layer (skeleton)" as new critical path ‚Äî migrations 046‚Äì050 are merged on main and the remaining 7 write handlers are fully captured by queued PRs #208 + #209, so no DB skeleton remains; (iii) `telecheck-app/progress.json` at revision 175 ‚Äî no progress.json exists in telecheck-app; the live progress.json lives in **telecheckONE** at revision 266. **Brief-vs-reality reconciliation (unchanged from Addenda 130‚Äì165).** This firing's brief again carried the same stale premises: (i) "most recent migration is 045" ‚Äî actual high-water is **052** (confirmed `ls migrations/` this firing); (ii) priority (a) "Med-Interaction slice DB layer (skeleton)" as new critical path ‚Äî migrations 046‚Äì050 are merged on main and the remaining 7 write handlers are fully captured by queued PRs #208 + #209, so no DB skeleton remains; (iii) `telecheck-app/progress.json` at revision 175 ‚Äî no progress.json exists in telecheck-app; the live progress.json lives in **telecheckONE** at revision 269. No work lost (the true remote state was restored intact).

**Context.** Standing remote-cron autonomous firing against the Master Completion Plan. Detected via the migration-high-water tripwire (the brief's "045" matched the stale checkout) ‚Üí re-probed `git ls-remote` (true tip `c27638c`) ‚Üí corrected via `git fetch origin main` + `git reset --hard c27638c`. Post-correction migration high-water confirmed **052**. Exact CLAUDE.md-documented trap; the high-water cross-check against Addendum 178's "052" surfaced it. |
| `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue | GitHub MCP `list_pull_requests` (state=open, perPage=50) ‚Äî live probe this firing | **All 9 intact + still `[CODEX-PENDING]`**: #199 (`485933b`, crisis acknowledge), #202 (`ffac0e1`, crisis respond+resolve), #203 (`62b1ddf`, crisis patient-summary read), #204 (`4464053`, crisis `_sweep`), #206 (`4db0961`, admin review-decision), #207 (`9f1fabe`, admin dashboard reads), #208 (`09a8712`, med-int 3 write handlers), #209 (`2b5c48a`, med-int 4 write handlers), #210 (`e24f830`, AI Mode 2 case-prep).
