Remaining 7 carry unchanged head SHAs from Addendum 178 ‚Üí CI runs against fixed SHAs cannot have changed. The one non-duplicative lever (CI-repair of a regressed member) has **no target** this firing. |
| Spec ratification (Phase B unblock) | GitHub MCP open-PR list ‚Äî SI docs PRs #137/#138/#139 | **Still pre-ratification.** #137 (SI-003 v0.2 `4e06115`), #138 (SI-004 v0.2 `a27d34e`), #139 (SI-005 v0.2 `5f3b68c`) all open + head SHAs byte-identical to Addendum 178. Ratification ceremony (Evans + Engineering Lead + CDM owner) is a STOP condition ‚Äî cannot be executed autonomously. Phase B remains BLOCKED-ON-RATIFIER. |
| Migration high-water | telecheck-app `ls migrations/` (post-stale-ref correction) | **052** (`052_admin_backend_submit_draft_state_guard.sql`) + full `rollback/` companion set through `052_rollback.sql`. Med-Interaction DB layer (046‚Äì050) is merged on main ‚Äî NOT a skeleton. |
| progress.json | telecheckONE (the brief's `telecheck-app/progress.json` + "175" remain STALE ‚Äî no progress.json exists in telecheck-app at all) | revision **282** (Addendum 178) ‚Üí bumped to **283** this firing. |

**Brief-vs-reality reconciliation (unchanged from Addenda 130‚Äì178).** This firing's brief again carried the same stale premises: (i) "most recent migration is 045" ‚Äî actual high-water is **052** (the brief's "045" matched my *stale local checkout* at `baca008` before the fetch-correction; the high-water cross-check against Addendum 178's "052" surfaced the trap); (ii) priority (a) "Med-Interaction slice DB layer (skeleton)" as new critical path ‚Äî migrations 046‚Äì050 merged on main and the remaining 7 write handlers fully captured by queued PRs #208 + #209, so no DB skeleton remains; (iii) `telecheck-app/progress.json` at revision 175 ‚Äî no progress.json exists in telecheck-app; the live progress.json lives in **telecheckONE** at revision 282. Priority (c) "AI Service Mode 1 chat handler wire-up" is **already MOUNTED on main**; only Mode 2 case-prep (#210) remains queued. Priority (b) async-consult clinician decision loop: depends on SI-005 ratification (#139 still open) ‚Äî blocked per the "spec ratification leads implementation by ‚â•1 sprint" discipline floor. Priority (d) crisis Sprint 2 ‚Üí #199/#202/#203/#204 queued; priority (e) admin Sprint 2 ‚Üí #206/#207 queued. All five brief-priority items map 1:1 onto the intact queue + the ratification blocker; all reconciled against primary sources.

**Why no net-new PR this firing.** All five brief-priority items (a)‚Äì(e) are either merged on main or fully captured by the intact 9-PR `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue, with priority (b) additionally ratification-gated (SI-005 #139). Re-authoring queued scope is forbidden duplication per the continuity-source discipline. Phase B fan-out is blocked on the SI-003/004/005 ratification ceremony (a STOP condition). No genuinely-new, non-duplicative, spec-ratified, buildable critical-path item exists. The single non-duplicative lever ‚Äî CI-repair of a regressed queue member ‚Äî has no target (fresh CI sample on #203 + #206 both 4/4-green; remaining 7 carry unchanged SHAs). Manufacturing a 10th PR that also cannot be Codex-reviewed would grow the queue without converging anything and risks duplicating queued scope. Held rather than manufactured make-work.

**Two blockers, both out-of-env / operator-gated:**
1. **Codex unavailable in this remote-cron env** ‚Üí the 9-PR queue cannot clear the mandatory-APPROVE merge gate. Resolves when a session runs with `OPENAI_API_KEY` set (Evans's local session, or a remote env provisioned with the key). At that point all 9 queued PRs can be Codex-reviewed round-by-round and merged on APPROVE.
2. **SI-003/004/005 ratification** (Phase B unblock) ‚Üí requires the ratifier ceremony (Evans + Engineering Lead + CDM owner). STOP condition; cannot be executed autonomously.

**Stale-ref traps ‚Äî BOTH repos PRESENT this firing; both handled.** telecheck-app: local HEAD lagged at `baca008` (69 commits behind true tip `c27638c`); the brief's stale "045" migration premise coincidentally matched the stale checkout. The cross-check against Addendum 178's recorded "052" surfaced the discrepancy ‚Üí `git ls-remote` confirmed true tip `c27638c` ‚Üí corrected via `git fetch origin main` + `git reset --hard c27638c`. **Lesson reinforced: trust `git ls-remote`, never the local tracking ref; cross-check migration high-water against the last Addendum as a stale-ref tripwire.** telecheckONE: container provisioned at detached HEAD == true remote tip `39cb519` (working tree = Addendum 178, progress 282) while local `main` *tracking* ref was stale at `6adaae5` (Addendum 71, progress 175). Per the documented lesson: did NOT `git checkout main` (which would revert the worktree to stale content); edited the worktree in place at the true tip, and landed on `main` via `git checkout -B main HEAD` before push.

**Loop-pacing recommendation ‚Äî held at PAUSE.** 48th consecutive Evans-gated parked firing; marginal value of each additional parked firing is near-zero (residual function is queue regression-detection, which a far slower cadence covers). Priority-ordered operator actions (all human-gated):
1. **PAUSE the remote-cron loop** until (2)/(3)/(4) lands.
2. **Provision Codex in the remote env**: set `OPENAI_API_KEY` (or mount a logged-in `~/.codex/auth.json`) AND open the network policy to `api.openai.com`. This alone would let a remote firing drain the 9-PR queue end-to-end.
3. **Drain the 9-PR `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue** in a Codex-enabled local session ‚Äî all CI-green on current main `c27638c`; nothing blocks the Codex‚Üímerge cascade but Codex availability.
4. **Run the CDM + SI-003/004/005 + Plan-v1.1 batched ratifier ceremony** (charter STOP condition ‚Äî Evans + Engineering Lead + CDM owner).

(ii) priority (a) "Med-Interaction slice DB layer (skeleton)" as new critical path ‚Äî migrations 046‚Äì050 merged on main and the remaining 7 write handlers fully captured by queued PRs #208 + #209, so no DB skeleton remains; (iii) `telecheck-app/progress.json` at revision 175 ‚Äî no progress.json exists in telecheck-app; the live progress.json lives in **telecheckONE** at revision 286. Priority (c) "AI Service Mode 1 chat handler wire-up" is **already MOUNTED on main**; only Mode 2 case-prep (#210) remains queued. Priority (b) async-consult clinician decision loop: depends on SI-005 ratification (#139 still open) ‚Äî blocked per the "spec ratification leads implementation by ‚â•1 sprint" discipline floor. Priority (d) crisis Sprint 2 ‚Üí #199/#202/#203/#204 queued; priority (e) admin Sprint 2 ‚Üí #206/#207 queued. All five brief-priority items map 1:1 onto the intact queue + the ratification blocker; all reconciled against primary sources.

**Why no net-new PR this firing.** All five brief-priority items (a)‚Äì(e) are either merged on main or fully captured by the intact 9-PR `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue, with priority (b) additionally ratification-gated (SI-005 #139). Re-authoring queued scope is forbidden duplication per the continuity-source discipline. Phase B fan-out is blocked on the SI-003/004/005 ratification ceremony (a STOP condition). No genuinely-new, non-duplicative, spec-ratified, buildable critical-path item exists. The single non-duplicative lever ‚Äî CI-repair of a regressed queue member ‚Äî has no target (fresh CI sample on #206 + #209 both 4/4-green; remaining 7 carry unchanged SHAs). Manufacturing a 10th PR that also cannot be Codex-reviewed would grow the queue without converging anything and risks duplicating queued scope. Held rather than manufactured make-work.

**Two blockers, both out-of-env / operator-gated:**
1. **Codex unavailable in this remote-cron env** ‚Üí the 9-PR queue cannot clear the mandatory-APPROVE merge gate. Resolves when a session runs with `OPENAI_API_KEY` set (Evans's local session, or a remote env provisioned with the key). At that point all 9 queued PRs can be Codex-reviewed round-by-round and merged on APPROVE.
2. **SI-003/004/005 ratification** (Phase B unblock) ‚Üí requires the ratifier ceremony (Evans + Engineering Lead + CDM owner). STOP condition; cannot be executed autonomously.

**Stale-ref traps ‚Äî app PRESENT (cosmetic for docs-only firing), telecheckONE PRESENT + handled this firing.** telecheck-app: container provisioned with HEAD at true remote tip `c27638c` (`git rev-parse HEAD` == `git ls-remote origin refs/heads/main`) but the local `main` *tracking* ref stale at `baca008` (69 commits behind). Because HEAD/worktree were already correct and this firing pushes no telecheck-app code, no `git reset --hard origin/main` was required; the stale tracking ref does not affect the docs-only artifacts. telecheckONE: container provisioned at detached HEAD == true remote tip `423371c` (working tree = Addendum 182, progress 286) while local `main` *tracking* ref was stale at `6adaae5` (Addendum 71, progress 175). Per the documented lesson: did NOT `git checkout main` (which would revert the worktree to stale content); edited the worktree in place at the true tip, and landed on `main` via `git checkout -B main HEAD` before push. **Lesson reinforced: trust `git ls-remote`, never the local tracking ref; cross-check migration high-water against the last Addendum as a stale-ref tripwire.**

**Loop-pacing recommendation ‚Äî held at PAUSE.** 52nd consecutive Evans-gated parked firing; marginal value of each additional parked firing is near-zero (residual function is queue regression-detection, which a far slower cadence covers). Priority-ordered operator actions (all human-gated):
1. **PAUSE the remote-cron loop** until (2)/(3)/(4) lands.
2. **Provision Codex in the remote env**: set `OPENAI_API_KEY` (or mount a logged-in `~/.codex/auth.json`) AND open the network policy to `api.openai.com`. This alone would let a remote firing drain the 9-PR queue end-to-end.
3. **Drain the 9-PR `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue** in a Codex-enabled local session ‚Äî all CI-green on current main `c27638c`; nothing blocks the Codex‚Üímerge cascade but Codex availability.
4. **Run the CDM + SI-003/004/005 + Plan-v1.1 batched ratifier ceremony** (charter STOP condition ‚Äî Evans + Engineering Lead + CDM owner).

**Context.** Standing remote-cron autonomous firing against the Master Completion Plan. Per the discipline floor, with Codex unavailable the firing's job is to (a) re-verify queue-health premises from primary sources (NOT inherited), (b) identify any genuinely-new, non-duplicative, spec-ratified, mergeable-or-PR-able critical-path item, and (c) if none exists, hold rather than manufacture make-work. This is the **55th consecutive firing** to land on the parked state (cf. Addenda 127‚Äì185). Trust-but-verify applied; every premise below was independently re-probed this firing.

**Primary-source verification table (all re-probed THIS firing):**

| Premise | Source-of-truth probe | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Codex availability | `[ -z "$OPENAI_API_KEY" ]` + `command -v codex` + `ls ~/.codex/auth.json /root/.codex/auth.json` + `curl --max-time 8 api.openai.com/v1/models` | **UNAVAILABLE ‚Äî unchanged from Addendum 185.** `OPENAI_API_KEY` UNSET; no `codex` binary on PATH; no `~/.codex/auth.json` or `/root/.codex/auth.json`; `api.openai.com/v1/models` ‚Üí **403**. No `codex review` can run from this env. |
| telecheck-app main | `git ls-remote origin refs/heads/main` + `git rev-parse HEAD` | **`c27638c`** ‚Äî unmoved since Addendum 130. **App stale-ref trap PRESENT this firing**: container provisioned with both the local `main` ref AND a detached HEAD at `baca008` (69 commits behind true remote tip `c27638c`); corrected via `git fetch origin main` + `git checkout -B main origin/main`. Clean tree after correction. A genuine delta from Addendum 185 where local HEAD/ref were already at the true tip on first probe. |
| `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue | GitHub MCP `list_pull_requests` (state=open, perPage=50) ‚Äî live probe this firing | **All 9 intact + still `[CODEX-PENDING]`**: #199 (`485933b`), #202 (`ffac0e1`), #203 (`62b1ddf`), #204 (`4464053`), #206 (`4db0961`), #207 (`9f1fabe`), #208 (`09a8712`), #209 (`2b5c48a`), #210 (`e24f830`). Head SHAs byte-identical to Addendum 185 ‚Üí nothing pushed/regressed since. None closed/merged. (Also open but out-of-critical-path: dependabot #214/#190/#189/#91/#90; stale sprint drafts #37/#32/#31.) |
| Queue CI health | GitHub MCP `get_check_runs` on #199 + #209 (varied sample vs Addendum 185's #207+#208) + unchanged-SHA inference on remaining 7 | **Sampled #199 and #209 both 4/4 CI-green** ("Build, lint, typecheck, test" + benchmarks + verify-metadata + dependency-review, all `success`). Remaining 7 carry unchanged head SHAs from Addendum 185 ‚Üí CI runs against fixed SHAs cannot have changed. The one non-duplicative lever (CI-repair of a regressed member) has **no target** this firing. |
| Spec ratification (Phase B unblock) | GitHub MCP open-PR list ‚Äî SI docs PRs #137/#138/#139 | **Still pre-ratification.** #137 (SI-003 v0.2 `4e06115`), #138 (SI-004 v0.2 `a27d34e`), #139 (SI-005 v0.2 `5f3b68c`) all open + head SHAs byte-identical to Addendum 185. Ratification ceremony (Evans + Engineering Lead + CDM owner) is a STOP condition ‚Äî cannot be executed autonomously. Phase B remains BLOCKED-ON-RATIFIER. |
| Migration high-water | telecheck-app `ls migrations/` (on synced main `c27638c`) | **052** (`052_admin_backend_submit_draft_state_guard.sql`) + full `rollback/` companion set 033‚Üí052. Med-Interaction DB layer (046‚Äì050) is merged on main ‚Äî NOT a skeleton. |
| progress.json | telecheckONE (the brief's `telecheck-app/progress.json` + "175" remain STALE ‚Äî no progress.json exists in telecheck-app at all) | revision **289** (Addendum 185) ‚Üí bumped to **290** this firing. |

**Brief-vs-reality reconciliation (unchanged from Addenda 130‚Äì185).** This firing's brief again carried the same stale premises: (i) "most recent migration is 045" ‚Äî actual high-water is **052**; (ii) priority (a) "Med-Interaction slice DB layer (skeleton)" as new critical path ‚Äî migrations 046‚Äì050 are merged on main and the remaining 7 write handlers are fully captured by queued PRs #208 + #209, so no DB skeleton remains; (iii) `telecheck-app/progress.json` at revision 175 ‚Äî no progress.json exists in telecheck-app; the live progress.json lives in **telecheckONE** at revision 289. Priority (c) "AI Service Mode 1 chat handler wire-up" is **already MOUNTED on main**; only Mode 2 case-prep (#210) remains queued. Priority (b) async-consult clinician decision loop depends on SI-005 ratification (#139 still open) ‚Äî blocked per the "spec ratification leads implementation by ‚â•1 sprint" discipline floor. Priority (d) crisis Sprint 2 ‚Üí #199/#202/#203/#204 queued; priority (e) admin Sprint 2 ‚Üí #206/#207 queued. All reconciled against primary sources.

**Why no net-new PR this firing.** All five brief-priority items (a)‚Äì(e) are either merged on main or fully captured by the intact 9-PR `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue, with priority (b) additionally ratification-gated (SI-005 #139). Re-authoring queued scope is forbidden duplication per the continuity-source discipline. Phase B fan-out is blocked on the SI-003/004/005 ratification ceremony (a STOP condition). No genuinely-new, non-duplicative, spec-ratified, buildable critical-path item exists. The single non-duplicative lever ‚Äî CI-repair of a regressed queue member ‚Äî has no target (fresh CI sample on #199 + #209 both 4/4-green; remaining 7 carry unchanged SHAs). Manufacturing a 10th PR that also cannot be Codex-reviewed would grow the queue without converging anything and risks duplicating queued scope. Held rather than manufactured make-work.

**Two blockers, both out-of-env / operator-gated:**
1. **Codex unavailable in this remote-cron env** ‚Üí the 9-PR queue cannot clear the mandatory-APPROVE merge gate. Resolves when a session runs with `OPENAI_API_KEY` set (Evans's local session, or a remote env provisioned with the key). At that point all 9 queued PRs can be Codex-reviewed round-by-round and merged on APPROVE.
2. **SI-003/004/005 ratification** (Phase B unblock) ‚Üí requires the ratifier ceremony (Evans + Engineering Lead + CDM owner). STOP condition; cannot be executed autonomously.

**Stale-ref traps ‚Äî both repos PRESENT this firing; handled.** telecheck-app: container provisioned with local `main` ref + detached HEAD both at `baca008` (69 commits behind); `git ls-remote` showed true tip `c27638c`; corrected via `git fetch origin main` + `git checkout -B main origin/main`. telecheckONE: container provisioned at detached HEAD == true remote tip `104532a` (working tree = Addendum 185, progress 289) while the local `main` *tracking* ref was stale at `6adaae5` (Addendum 71, progress 175). Per the documented lesson: did NOT `git checkout main` (which would revert the worktree to stale content); edited the worktree in place at the true tip, and landed on `main` via `git checkout -B main HEAD` before push.

**Loop-pacing recommendation ‚Äî held at PAUSE.** 55th consecutive Evans-gated parked firing; marginal value of each additional parked firing is near-zero (residual function is queue regression-detection, which a far slower cadence covers). Priority-ordered operator actions (all human-gated):
1. **PAUSE the remote-cron loop** until (2)/(3)/(4) lands.
2. **Provision Codex in the remote env**: set `OPENAI_API_KEY` (or mount a logged-in `~/.codex/auth.json`) AND open the network policy to `api.openai.com`. This alone would let a remote firing drain the 9-PR queue end-to-end.
3. **Drain the 9-PR `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue** in a Codex-enabled local session ‚Äî all CI-green on current main `c27638c`; nothing blocks the Codex‚Üímerge cascade but Codex availability.
4. **Run the CDM + SI-003/004/005 + Plan-v1.1 batched ratifier ceremony** (charter STOP condition ‚Äî Evans + Engineering Lead + CDM owner).

Priority (e) admin Sprint 2 ‚Üí submit + crisis-dashboard merged on main; decision + dashboards queued as #206/#207.

Re-authoring queued scope is forbidden duplication per the continuity-source discipline. Phase B fan-out is blocked on the SI-003/004/005 ratification ceremony (a STOP condition); Plan v1.1 ¬ß3 re-read this firing confirms Phase A RATIFIER-READY (ceremony-gated) + Phase B BLOCKED-ON-RATIFIER (OQ2-gated). The three candidate non-duplicative levers all remain non-actionable: (1) **CI-repair of a regressed queue member** ‚Äî fresh CI sample on #210 4/4-green, remaining 8 carry unchanged SHAs, so no regressed target; (2) **net-new endpoint not yet in the queue** ‚Äî every brief-named endpoint is merged-or-queued; (3) **net-new handler in a slice with queued work** ‚Äî confirmed in Addendum 192 via #208 file-list (queued PRs edit the shared per-slice `routes.ts`), so any new handler would conflict-and-stack on a base that cannot merge until Codex returns. **Brief-vs-reality reconciliation (unchanged from Addenda 130‚Äì194).** This firing's brief again carried the same stale premises, all reconciled against primary sources this firing: (i) "most recent migration is 045" ‚Äî actual high-water is **052**; (ii) priority (a) "Med-Interaction slice DB layer (skeleton)" as new critical path ‚Äî migrations 046‚Äì050 are merged on main and the remaining write handlers are fully captured by queued PRs #208 + #209, so no DB skeleton remains; (iii) `telecheck-app/progress.json` at revision 175 ‚Äî no progress.json exists in telecheck-app; the live progress.json lives in **telecheckONE** at revision 298; (iv) the status-doc + progress.json paths in the brief are given as `telecheck-app/...` but both actually live in **telecheckONE**. Priority (c) "AI Service Mode 1 chat handler wire-up" is **already MOUNTED on main**; only Mode 2 case-prep (#210) remains queued. Priority (b) async-consult clinician decision loop depends on SI-005 ratification (#139 still open) ‚Äî blocked per the "spec ratification leads implementation by ‚â•1 sprint" discipline floor. Priority (d) crisis Sprint 2 ‚Üí initiate/acknowledge merged on main; respond/resolve/sweep/patient-summary queued as #199/#202/#203/#204. Priority (e) admin Sprint 2 ‚Üí submit + crisis-dashboard merged on main; decision + dashboards queued as #206/#207.

**Why no net-new PR this firing.** All five brief-priority items (a)‚Äì(e) are either merged on main or fully captured by the intact 9-PR `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue, with priority (b) additionally ratification-gated (SI-005 #139). Re-authoring queued scope is forbidden duplication per the continuity-source discipline. Phase B fan-out is blocked on the SI-003/004/005 ratification ceremony (a STOP condition). The three candidate non-duplicative levers all remain non-actionable: (1) **CI-repair of a regressed queue member** ‚Äî fresh CI sample on #209 + #207 both 4/4-green, remaining 7 carry unchanged SHAs, so no regressed target; (2) **net-new endpoint not yet in the queue** ‚Äî every brief-named endpoint is merged-or-queued; (3) **net-new handler in a slice with queued work** ‚Äî queued PRs edit the shared per-slice `routes.ts` (confirmed Addendum 192 via #208 file-list), so any new handler would conflict-and-stack on a base that cannot merge until Codex returns. No genuinely-new, non-duplicative, spec-ratified, buildable critical-path item exists. Manufacturing a 10th PR that also cannot be Codex-reviewed would grow the queue without converging anything. Held rather than manufactured make-work.

**Two blockers, both out-of-env / operator-gated:**
1. **Codex unavailable in this remote-cron env** ‚Üí the 9-PR queue cannot clear the mandatory-APPROVE merge gate. Resolves when a session runs with `OPENAI_API_KEY` set (Evans's local session, or a remote env provisioned with the key). At that point all 9 queued PRs can be Codex-reviewed round-by-round and merged on APPROVE.
2. **SI-003/004/005 ratification** (Phase B unblock) ‚Üí requires the ratifier ceremony (Evans + Engineering Lead + CDM owner). STOP condition; cannot be executed autonomously.

**Stale-ref traps ‚Äî telecheck-app PARTIAL, telecheckONE PRESENT this firing; both handled.** telecheck-app: container provisioned at detached HEAD AND worktree already at the true tip `c27638c` on first probe (clean, high-water 052, no worktree correction needed); the local `main` branch ref + `origin/main` tracking ref lagged at `baca008` (69 behind), corrected `origin/main` via `git fetch origin main`. telecheckONE: container provisioned at detached HEAD == true remote tip `eef80d10` (working tree = Addendum 194, progress 298) while the local `main` *tracking* ref was stale at `6adaae5` (Addendum 71, progress 175). Per the documented lesson: did NOT `git checkout main` (which would revert the worktree to stale content); edited the worktree in place at the true tip, landing on `main` via `git checkout -B main HEAD` before push.

**Loop-pacing recommendation ‚Äî held at PAUSE.** 64th consecutive Evans-gated parked firing; marginal value of each additional parked firing is near-zero (residual function is queue regression-detection, which a far slower cadence covers). Priority-ordered operator actions (all human-gated):
1. **PAUSE the remote-cron loop** until (2)/(3)/(4) lands.
2. **Provision Codex in the remote env**: set `OPENAI_API_KEY` (or mount a logged-in `~/.codex/auth.json`) AND open the network policy to `api.openai.com`. This alone would let a remote firing drain the 9-PR queue end-to-end.
3. **Drain the 9-PR `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue** in a Codex-enabled local session ‚Äî all CI-green on current main `c27638c`; nothing blocks the Codex‚Üímerge cascade but Codex availability.
4. **Run the CDM + SI-003/004/005 + Plan-v1.1 batched ratifier ceremony** (charter STOP condition ‚Äî Evans + Engineering Lead + CDM owner).

**Why no net-new PR this firing.** All five brief-priority items (a)‚Äì(e) are either merged on main or fully captured by the intact 9-PR `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue, with priority (b) additionally ratification-gated (SI-005 superseded-for-ratification by SI-020 Sub-decision 1; SI-005 PR #139 still open). Re-authoring queued scope is forbidden duplication per the continuity-source discipline. Phase B fan-out is blocked on the SI-003/004/005 ratification ceremony (a STOP condition). The three candidate non-duplicative levers all remain non-actionable: (1) **CI-repair of a regressed queue member** ‚Äî fresh CI sample on #199 4/4-green, remaining 8 carry unchanged SHAs, so no regressed target; (2) **net-new endpoint not yet in the queue** ‚Äî every brief-named endpoint is merged-or-queued; (3) **net-new handler in a slice with queued work** ‚Äî queued PRs edit the shared per-slice `routes.ts`, so any new handler would conflict-and-stack on a base that cannot merge until Codex returns. No genuinely-new, non-duplicative, spec-ratified, buildable critical-path item exists. Manufacturing a 10th PR that also cannot be Codex-reviewed would grow the queue without converging anything. Held rather than manufactured make-work.

**Brief stale-premise reconciliation (re-confirmed THIS firing).** The firing brief's premises remain stale and were each re-checked against primary sources: migration "045" ‚Üí actually **052** (`ls migrations/`); Med-Interaction "DB skeleton / new critical path" ‚Üí DB layer (migrations 046‚Äì050) **already merged on main**, write handlers queued as #208/#209; progress "175" in `telecheck-app/progress.json` ‚Üí no such file in telecheck-app; the real file is `telecheckONE/progress.json` at revision **304**; Mode 1 chat handler "wire-up" ‚Üí **already merged** (Mode 2 case-prep queued as #210). The brief's priority ladder (a)‚Äì(e) is fully merged-or-queued.

**Loop-pacing recommendation ‚Äî held at PAUSE.** 70th consecutive Evans-gated parked firing; marginal value of each additional parked firing is near-zero (residual function is queue regression-detection, which a far slower cadence covers). Priority-ordered operator actions (all human-gated):

**Brief-vs-reality reconciliation (unchanged from Addenda 130‚Äì222).** This firing's brief again carried the same stale premises: (i) "most recent migration is 045" ‚Äî actual high-water is **052**; (ii) priority (a) "Med-Interaction slice DB layer (skeleton)" as new critical path ‚Äî migrations 046‚Äì050 are merged on main and remaining write handlers are fully captured by queued PRs #208 + #209, no DB skeleton remains; (iii) `telecheck-app/progress.json` at revision 175 ‚Äî no progress.json exists in telecheck-app; the live progress.json lives in **telecheckONE** at revision 326 ‚Üí bumped to 327 this firing. **Why no net-new PR this firing.** All five brief-priority items (a)‚Äì(e) are either merged on main or fully captured by the intact 9-PR `[CODEX-PENDING]` queue, with priority (b) additionally ratification-gated (SI-005 #139 still open). Re-authoring queued scope is forbidden duplication per the continuity-source discipline. Phase B fan-out is blocked on the SI-003/004/005 ratification ceremony (a STOP condition). No genuinely-new, non-duplicative, spec-ratified, buildable critical-path item exists. The single non-duplicative lever ‚Äî CI-repair of a regressed queue member ‚Äî has no target (fresh CI sample on #204 4/4-green; remaining 8 carry unchanged SHAs since Addendum 222). Manufacturing a 10th PR that also cannot be Codex-reviewed would grow the queue without converging anything and risks duplicating queued scope. Held rather than manufactured make-work.

**Two blockers, both out-of-env / operator-gated:**
1. **Codex unavailable in this remote-cron env** ‚Üí the 9-PR queue cannot clear the mandatory-APPROVE merge gate. Resolves when a session runs with `OPENAI_API_KEY` set (Evans's local session, or a remote env provisioned with the key). At that point all 9 queued PRs can be Codex-reviewed round-by-round and merged on APPROVE.

The single non-duplicative lever ‚Äî CI-repair of a regressed queue member ‚Äî has no target (fresh CI sample on #206 4/4-green; remaining 8 carry unchanged SHAs since Addendum 243). Manufacturing a 10th PR that also cannot be Codex-reviewed would grow the queue without converging anything and risks duplicating queued scope. Held rather than manufactured make-work.

**Two blockers, both out-of-env / operator-gated:**
1. **Codex unavailable in this remote-cron env** ‚Üí the 9-PR queue cannot clear the mandatory-APPROVE merge gate. Resolves when a session runs with `OPENAI_API_KEY` set AND network policy opens api.openai.com (Evans's local session, or a remote env provisioned with both). At that point all 9 queued PRs can be Codex-reviewed round-by-round and merged on APPROVE.
2. **SI-003/004/005 ratification** (Phase B unblock) ‚Üí requires the ratifier ceremony (Evans + Engineering Lead + CDM owner). STOP condition; cannot be executed autonomously.

**Codex-availability sub-probe (firing #141):** in addition to the `OPENAI_API_KEY` length-0 + `codex-cli 0.135.0` reachable findings standing since A130, this firing surfaced one fresh observation worth recording: the telecheckONE CLAUDE.md "Codex adversarial-review cadence" section invokes the Codex companion at `C:/Users/menso/.claude/plugins/cache/openai-codex/codex/1.0.4/scripts/codex-companion.mjs` ‚Äî a Windows-host path (Evans's local machine) inaccessible from this Linux container regardless of auth wall. Even if the `OPENAI_API_KEY` were provisioned in this env, the companion-script invocation path documented as canonical in CLAUDE.md cannot be reached from container fs ‚Äî so the new dual-recommendation + two-pass + auto-proceed cycle disciplines codified in A211+ via Evans's 2026-05-20 directives (which all hinge on Codex Pass-1/Pass-2 invocation through the companion script) are doubly unavailable in this remote-cron env. The npx `@openai/codex` direct-CLI path remains available but the structured consult-framing prompts in CLAUDE.md target the companion-script entrypoint specifically, so even a direct-CLI fallback would not match the codified discipline shape. This widens the parked-state-premise reasoning: Codex unavailability in this remote-cron env is not just an auth-wall issue but an entrypoint-architectural issue tied to the companion-script's Windows-only canonical path. Mitigation paths would require either (a) `OPENAI_API_KEY` provisioning + adaptation of the consult prompts to the npx direct-CLI shape, or (b) a Linux-host companion-script port ‚Äî neither of which is in-scope for autonomous remote-cron action under the discipline floor.

The network-policy allowlist on this remote-execution container does NOT include `api.openai.com` ‚Äî the auth-wall surface is "host-not-allowlisted" not "key-missing-or-invalid," meaning even an `OPENAI_API_KEY` set in this env would still 403 on the websocket handshake. **CI is green only because every crisis test fixture uses UUID-shaped literals like `00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000001` (36 chars ‚Äî which cannot even be stored in `accounts.account_id VARCHAR(26)`) and the handlers mock `current_actor_account_id()` / the actor context, so the real ULID‚ÜîUUID mismatch is never exercised**; ## Addendum 330 ‚Äî 2026-07-06 ‚Äî BOTH TENANTS RESOLVING ON STAGING: multi-tenant request path verified end-to-end over public HTTPS

**Delivered:** `https://87.99.159.214.sslip.io` ‚Üí Telecheck-US and `https://ghana.87.99.159.214.sslip.io` ‚Üí Telecheck-Ghana both resolve tenant context and enforce auth (401 fail-closed on `/v1/async-consults/queue` without JWT; module readiness endpoints reporting honest gated status). Verified chain per host: Caddy TLS (per-host Let's Encrypt certs) ‚Üí Fastify ‚Üí tenant-context resolution (DB-authoritative) ‚Üí auth hook ‚Üí module routes.

**PRs #239 + #240 (TENANT_HOST_OVERRIDES):** staging hostnames aren't canonical consumer subdomains, so resolution 400'd. #239 added env-driven `hostname=TenantId` aliases (boot-fail-fast parsing; canonical tenants only; 12 unit tests) + Caddy `STAGING_DOMAINS` multi-host support. #240 completed it: in production the static map is deliberately dead (foundation-wiring-r2/r3 closures ‚Äî DB authoritative), so the override now redirects the authoritative DB lookup itself (`WHERE id = <aliased tenant>` instead of `WHERE consumer_subdomain = <host>`), preserving every fail-closed property (status='active' gate, country union check, tri-state semantics, no tenant minting, deactivated tenant stops resolving).

**Env-file gotcha recorded:** `STAGING_DOMAINS` contains a space ‚Äî must be quoted in `infra/staging/.env` because deploy.sh `source`s the file (docker-compose env-file parsing doesn't care, bash does).

**Root `/ready` observation:** there is no root-level `/ready` route ‚Äî readiness is per-module (`/v0/<module>/ready`, honest `slice_hardening_pending` reasons). A root aggregate readiness endpoint is a small follow-up for LB health checks at AWS pre-go-live.

**Staging state after this arc:** infra COMPLETE for pilot-scope testing ‚Äî multi-tenant HTTPS, PG16 TLS, 61 migrations, Redis, SI-010 bind pool, both tenants live. **Next critical-path item: staging JWT issuance (test tokens per role) ‚Üí authenticated E2E consult-flow smoke (initiate ‚Üí intake ‚Üí queue ‚Üí claim ‚Üí decision) against Telecheck-US.**
